name: MARY CORNELIA ABNEY
motto: “Sincerity in word and deed is more precious than jewels.”
description: Cornelia came to the University two years ago from Lucy Cobb and is a student of whom any institution would be glad to boast. She is always ready to start some fun, but her lessons never suffer from it. She has not failed to take an active and prominent part in college activities and has made an enviable record in everything that she has done. She has a sunny smile and a winning manner which make friends for her wherever she goes. She has a lovable nature and can be depended on to do thoroughly anything that she undertakes. Cornelia, we can not predict what the future has in store for you, but we know that you will meet life squarely and will always be successful in everything you undertake. We are sorry that you are leaving, but we know that you will be an alumna that Georgia will be proud to claim.

name: JAMES THOMAS ADAIR
motto: “Do as you would be done by.”
description: Tom Adair is a product of which Athens can be truly proud. While he hasn't set the woods a-fire with marks, he has set a standard for consistency that anyone would do well to emulate. For four years this young man has come and gone on the campus, speaking to all but making few acquaintances, yet those acquaintances which he has made have been real friends. His philosophy of life, found at the bottom of this article, is a good criterion of his character. Tom has most impressed us by the many different girls he seems to take out “flivvering.” Everytime you see him he has a different member of the “aggravating sex” at his side. It is rumored that he has only to whistle, or make a noise like a chocolate milk and the ladies will gather round him like piccaninnies around a watermelou. That is rumor, however, and there will be rumors and rumors, etc. We don't know what Tom is planning to do when he gets out of college, but we would be willing to bet our first three months salary that whatever it is he does it well. May good fortune follow you always, old man.

name: Grace Allen
motto: "Neither to laugh nor to cry, but to understand."
description: Grace is a gift to us from Wesleyan. She arrived in the embryonic stage and has developed into a great specimen. Her life history has been very complex, displaying a heterogeneous phenotype of an allelomorphic nature. Her customary habitat is the Co-ed Building, but she has often been found about LeConte Hall, and sometimes even at Mano's Weiner Stand. She seems to have inherited a tendency to overcome environment, and since her advent here has been rapidly scaling the heights of success. It was she, who was largely responsible for the installment of the Chromosome Theory of Student Government for Women. She has also done a lot of good work among the churches of Athens, especially in keeping the pot boiling for Foreign Missions. By her constant study and thought, Grace has not only started herself upon the road to fame, but her excellent example has been the inspiration to more than one girl or boy to strive to reach a goal of the highest culture through the pursuit of Christian principles.

name: Thurston Lafayette Anthony
motto: "The world cares not who your ancestors were—What it wants to know is, 'Who are you?'"
description: Friends, Romans, Countrymen, lend me your ears. What manner of man is this? He stands six feet two inches tall and weighs 200 pounds, and is the mightiest of the mighty in physical ability. He was in active service in France for twelve months during the World War, and participated in all the major engagements of the Eighty-second division. There he won a medal proclaiming him the champion all-round athlete of his division. While Mark is idolized by all supporters of Georgia's athletics for devoting his entire energy to the glory of Georgia, even to the point of sacrifice, yet he is known by his closest friends not merely as an athlete but as an all-round good fellow. He is always willing to aid a friend in need of help—but one need never go to him with his dirty political schemes.

name: JOHN WILLIAM BENNETT, JR.
motto: "They never taste who always drink, They always talk who never think."
enrolltime: During his four years at Georgia
description: "Johnnie" came to us as the younger of two Bennetts and has made a record of which he is justly proud. During his four years at Georgia he has established himself as one of the best men in college. He has taken part in almost every activity of worth, and has invariably come out a stronger man because of the time thus spent. "Johnnie" says that one of his greatest assets, and by the way, the one which always makes him succeed, is found in the fact that he is very fond of one of the fairer sex. Forgetting all of the genial traits of his personality we would say that in this man we find strength of character, a delightful personality, and one of the strongest touches of individuality that we have felt in many a day.

name: JOSEPH EDGAR CHAPMAN, JR.
motto: "Perseverance conquers all things."
description: In the fall of 1918 there came a certain young man, who was destined to gain much fame during his four years sojourn at Georgia. Joe has made an enviable record, which is clearly shown by the fact that he is a member of Junior Cabinet and Senior Round Table; he is in addition a worthy candidate for Phi Beta Kappa. Like Alexander of old, Joe is always seeking new worlds to conquer; he therefore journeyed over to the Law Department this year and succeeded in conquering the hitherto invincible Sylvanus. Ladies, it is useless to cast longing eyes at our Joseph, for he is a profound woman hater. Dame Rumor says that Joe is going to—well, it is a family secret. Joe, may all of your ambitions become realities!

name: FRANCES LUCILE CHUMBLEY
motto: "What one thinks of me matters little; what one knows of me matters much."
description: "Doc," the pioneer co-ed of Georgia, is taking with her the best wishes from all students. She has also won deserved honors, scholastic and athletic. Who but "Doc" could possess a disposition so friendly, so funloving, and so wholeheartedly genuine? There is no pretense about her; she is sincere in every thought and action and is boyishly frank at all times. Skill in music, sports, studies and household arts, combine to make her an "all-round" girl. Incidentally, "Doc" has shown such a preference for the "Classic City" that she has decided to return next year.

name: ROBERT MATHER DAVANT
motto: "Read 'em and laugh."
description: Another Candler Hall tea hound! At least, that is what "Bob" calls himself. He doesn't stay late but he goes so often. "Bob" has been more than a "ladies man," however, since he came to college—he "rates" in the military department. It is reported that "Bob" on the third trial induced hard-boiled Chastain to count off without calling the company to attention. He not only "rates" in the military department but possesses a wonderful knack of bootlicking the profs, especially in the physics department where he is mentioned both as a shark and as a pet. "Bob" may be guilty of some of the above things, but nevertheless one of the most honest, clean, upright men that ever came to Georgia. He is a hard worker, a clear thinker, and a noble gentleman. We predict that in whatever phase of life work he chooses to follow his influence will be felt.

name: JOHN WEYMAN DAVIS, B.S.
motto: "It's a waste of energy to run in low gear."
description: Ladies, look him over! We present for your approval Mr. John Weyman Davis of Athens, Georgia. Weyman lives in the "Classic City" and is proud of the fact that he does. He is our great social worker. It is rumored that he hasn't missed a Student Volunteer's Convention since that time in the dim and distant past when Dean Dudley wore pinafores. As usual, we feel that we must assert that Weyman is a "ladies man." To know him is to love him, or so the co-eds say, and we suppose that they are the ones to judge. But they don't know our Weyman. Love, in his vocabulary, is a violently active verb, punctuated by many whisperings of sweet nothings into the shell-like ear of any fair damsel that crosses his path. Mr. Davis excels at 'most anything, but his forte is straight shooting, being captain of the rifle team. He shoots the bull straight, he shoots the rifle straight, he is a great aid to Cupid in guiding his darts straight, and he shoots the profs straight. In fact, Weyman is straight in everything. Here's to you, Weyman!

name: AUSTIN FORD DEAN, A.B.
motto: "Positive anything is better than negative nothing."
description: Three years ago Austin entered the University, coming from the "Queen City of the Mountains." After a remarkable short time here, his ability was recognized. Although finishing in three years, he has entered to a marked degree in almost every phase of college activity; in the class-room he is unexcelled; as a debater he is feared by all opponents; and on the stage he is equalled by few amateurs. Within the walls of Phi Kappa Austin will long be remembered for his fiery eloquence and wonderful orations. Some students excell in a few fields, but he has excelled in every field which he has entered. We have often wondered what is the cause of such success, but Austin has at last told us in his philosophy of life. We know that in whatever field Austin follows in life he will succeed, because he is of the material of which success is made.

name: EDGAR MULLINS DUNSTAN, B.S.
motto: "In the attainment of the worth while, the surest foundation on which to build is sincerity."
description: "Senor" blew in from somewhere in the wilds of Brazil three years ago with great determination to learn something about everything. After one year spent in rambling about however, he decided that science was better suited to his temperament and therefore selected the Physics Department as his place of business, where he has steadily risen from blank wonder at the marvels of acceleration to an absolute understanding of the intricacies of astronomy. During "Senor's" stay with us he has made about everything there is to make, as the above list of honors will testify. And the greatest thing he has made has been friends; friends drawn to him and bound there by his personal qualities of true manhood—that's "Senor." "Senor," may your success in your home country be as meteoric as your career among us.

name: FRANK JENNINGS ERWIN, A.B.
motto: "To really live is rare; most people just exist."
clubs: First Lieut. M. T. C. Cadets
description: Ladies look him over! This is the one and only Frank Erwin, born in the mountains of North Georgia, but a citizen of the world at large. If you don't like our Frank, you don't know a good thing when you see it, and should be compelled to do without the society of men of his stamp. Frank doesn't smoke, drink or chew, and is therefore wont to seek recreation in the presence of any member of the contrary sex. They say that his line is so thick that he frequently suffocates them. But we don't believe it is his line that makes the final score, for Frank Jennings is extremely adept at drawing room wrestling. Now don't get the idea that our hero is forever placing an ear to the ground listening for the call of a damsel in distress, for he is not. Oh, no! He takes time to make all the debates that he wishes to make, and to accumulate all the honors that the law allows, besides making marks that are worthy of emulation by any student. Here's to you Frank! May you ever carry your load as lightly as you have since we've known you.

name: EDWIN MALLARD EVERETT, A.B.
clubs: S. I. A. A. Tennis Tournament
description: "Ed" may need an introduction to many students of this University, for, although he has made many important honors, he has been sadly negligent in calling others' attention to this fact. Let us, therefore, present Mr. Everett of Atlanta, whose most salient characteristic is, perhaps, his scholarship. His industriousness is manifest from even a casual glance at his career here—in three years he has completed with excellent grades a four year course; he has become one of Georgia's mainstays in tennis; and has served as Editor-in-Chief of the Cracker for a half year. This survey shows clearly that "Ed's" time has been very fully taken up—furthermore this same survey evidences his great versatility and proves him an uncommon type in our University. He differs in still other ways from the typical college boy—he very frankly doesn't regard friendship so indifferently as to number his friends by his acquaintances.

name: WILLIAM CORNELIUS FLEMING, A.B. S.S.
motto: "Logical consequence are the scarecrows of fools, and the beacons of wise men."
description: "I'm from old U. Va.." said the Golden Tornado "breezing" his way towards Peabody Hall. Yes, "Red" went to Virginia where he acquired his great prestige, but after trying it for a year he wisely decided to complete his education at Georgia. And so for the past two years he has devoted himself with unceasing effort and unsurpassed interest to raising the standards of the School of Psychology, and making hard courses out of "crips." His efforts have not been in vain, for he has established a scholastic record which any of us would be proud to possess. Not confining himself to the University, "Red" burst forth with all his radiance into the Athens social world, and he has continued to shine as one of its leading lights. We hope that in the years to come this beacon of honor, industry, and good will, will show the way for his friends and compatriots.

name: FRANK H. FULLER, B.S.
description: "Tillie" hails from the plains of Culloden and prides himself on being one of the lucky graduates of the Georgia Military College. During his four-year stay with us this man has taken about every course offered on the third floor of the Academic Building. Fuller has a keen sense of humor and is truly one of the most intellectual men in his class. Tho. not one of the leading lights in college activities he possesses those elements of stability and culture which distinguish him from the multitudes. Fuller is one of the few men that we know who has a real and genuine appreciation for real literature. When all is said we believe that we have a man here who will grow old gracefully.

name: SARAH NELLE GILLAND, A.B. Ed.
motto: "To thine own self be true; Thou canst not then be false to any man."
clubs: Member of Y. W. C. A. Cabinet '22
Pres. Pres. Y. W. C. A. '22-'23
description: Who was it a long time ago that said something like this: "Some people are born great; others achieve greatness; while others have greatness thrust upon them?" Whoever it was forgot to mention a fourth class—those who become great through a combination of these three. To this fourth class our Nelle belongs. The many honors that have been hers during her two years at Georgia proclaim more successfully than can mere words the love and respect which her fellow students have for her and we predict that in her future career she will continue to win first place in the hearts of her associates. Quiet and dignified is this sage of the Class of '23. In all her college life she has proved to be the ideal "Georgia Co-ed" and we believe that in the future no member of this class will reflect more honor on our dear Alma Mater than Nelle.

name: James Keller Harper
motto: "Behind a will is a might."
enrolltime: During the four years that he has been with us
description: "Doc!" When this name is spoken there arises within us an emotion which words fall short of expressing—an admiration which only those who know him best can feel. During the four years that he has been with us his manly characteristics and "will-to-do" have won for him a place in every branch of college life. Since the first day that Georgia claimed him as her own, she has recognized this son as one who, by his influence, would lay a permanent stepping stone to her future success. Although his honors are numerous they fail to measure his success, for his most lasting work is manifested in those things which the printed page can not portray. Here is a loyal son, a true friend, and a man! "Doc," we predict for you a glorious future, and should Fortune smile upon you as we wish, your cup of life will be filled to overflowing.

name: Zachariah Clark Hayes, Jr.
motto: "Success don't konsist in never makin' blunders, but in never makin' the same one twict."
description: Zach hails from where the golden apples grow. Four years ago Zach brought to the University one of the most pleasing personalities that a man could have enshrined in himself. On entering school Zach set his goal, and today he has reached it, but not without failure knocking him down a few times. The old determination and fight won for him, however, and he has achieved much. As to honors Zach has taken them through sheer determination. No one who knew him as a freshman would have predicted that he would one day occupy the exalted position of Chairman of the Debating Council. And furthermore, who would have connected him with athletics? Still the team says that he made a good manager. Now, as a scholar, he looks the part and as such is good. In all your triumphs Zach, Old Man, you've made friends, and after you hit life's trail we see no reason why you can not achieve greater things. For with your personality, determination, and ability to make friends, you have God's greatest gift to man. We look to you, Zach!

name: CHARLES GOODRICH HENRY
motto: "If a little knowledge is a dangerous thing, Where is the man who knows enough to be out of danger?"
description: Many years ago, in the times when men valued God and placed less stress upon worldly matters, a star was seen in the heavens by three wise men. Today a "star" of a different type has been seen—not by three men alone, but by the entire University faculty and all those Georgia students who have had the privilege and pleasure of knowing "Slim" Henry. He is a man who may be aptly called the ideal college student, but one who is as far from the typical college student as the East is from the West. As to the true nature of Goodrich's character it is hard to state; we shall say, however, that he is morally upright, religiously inclined, and studious; the girls say that he is "indifferent," the boys that he is "ambitious," and the Co-eds that he is "charming."

name: FREEMAN CHEYNE McCLURE
motto: "Patient and persevering work is the only sure key to success."
clubs: Editorial Staff of Red and Black
description: "Senator" McClure, our only student legislator, came to enter himself as a student in this venerable institution three years ago. Since the eventful day when he was first a freshman, "Senator" has held the respect and esteem of faculty and students alike. "Senator" has distinguished himself in four of the main lines of student endeavor. In scholarship he is among the leaders of his class; in debating his record is among the very best; in politics he is in his element because of his natural leadership of men; and last, but by no means least, in the social life of the University he occupies a very prominent position. With his inherent ability, and the perseverance already demonstrated there is no reason why "Senator" should not win success in whatever line he takes up.

name: FREDERICK OSCAR McKENZIE, A.B.
motto: "One glance into a brilliant future, Then hitch your wagon to a star."
description: The voice of Henry Grady no longer echoes in the historic halls of Phi Kappa Literary Society. Robert Toombs, Alexander H. Stephens, and a host of other eminent Southern speakers have passed on, never again to charm the people with their eloquence and oratorical ability. It is not very often that the University produces men of such caliber as those, who make up the list of our immortals. But every year we are producing, here on our campus, speakers who will sway the political sentiments and public opinion of our future state, lesser satellites of the greater stars that adorn our alumnial firmament. Forensic training is indispensable in making leaders of men. Some years ago, a shrine was erected to Thalius, Muse of the Dramatic Art, when the University donned the buckskin and founded the Thalian Dramatic Club. Fred will long be remembered as an ardent follower and supreme exponent of these two worthy arts, as a man who takes part in all worthwhile activities, and as a man who, having proved his ability to lead college men, will be called to leadership in afterlife.

name: OSCAR ROLLIN METHVIN, JR., A.B.
motto: "The thorns we encounter in life's path only make the roses sweeter."
description: Oscar has the distinction of being one of the few of whom it may be said, "They never give up." He blew in from Dexter and there seemed to be a place waiting for him. He has made a great many friends at Georgia, and has been rather an exception, in that he has not tried to use them politically. Oscar has put in his spare time on the Track Team, making the Cross Country Team the last two years. He has also done excellent work as a student as well as in athletics and we will no doubt hear great things from him in the years to come. We must admit, however, that Oscar is quite a ladies man. Luck to you Oscar, old man. We believe that the future has much in store for you.

name: JAMES MOONEY, A. B.
description: Contrary to the prevalent local custom, Jim has had no advertising agency in college except his high type of work, which, of itself, is proclamation enough. Forced from school by sickness during his second year, he returned to finish four years work in what amounts to three. At the same time he has managed to contribute, while editor of the CRACKER, enough time and effort to be influential in that magazine's materializing as a first-class college publication. While doing this he has maintained a standard of scholarship that is among the best. Jim, to his own delight, has acquired the reputation of being a radical. Few persons, however, are so gifted with potential mental initiative as to warrant such a complimentary accusation. Jim, then, is to be inferred, is a little different, and in this case the departure from the mean is in the direction of superlative ability.

name: I. PAUL MORRIS, B.S.
description: Gentlemen, we present for your consideration another "local talent" specimen, Mr. I. P. Morris. Paul came to the University three years ago and during his sojourn has made quite an enviable record for himself. Ever since he has been in college he has been a good student, but not a book worm by any means. Wherever he goes he is generally in a big hurry and he usually gets there. He is usually to be seen where least expected and is an absolute authority on everything evolved from Terrell Hall. Being of a scientific mind, he has made a very brilliant record in chemistry. But chemistry is not the only thing in which he is good; he weilds a tennis racket, and is indeed an imposing spectacle in his Buick sedan, altho, he does not wear tortoise shell glasses. Lately it has been noticed that he is indulging in the theories of Ethics as advocated by Dr. Hutchinson. This makes it hard for us to predict his future but since he is going to follow the medical profession, we know that he will achieve honors in this field wherever he may hang out his sign.

name: CLARENCE MARION PAULK, A.B.
motto: "Knowledge is worth nothing unless we do the good we know."
enrolltime: with us for only two years
description: The man above is probably not as well known on the campus as some others for he has been with us for only two years. His face is that of a worker—one who goes out for everything. His battles are won and lost with good grace, he having long since learned that it is not the outcome but the fight that counts. Among the personalities much missed at Georgia next year will be his, for in his short time here he has made a goodly number of close friends. Clarence intends, we understand, to pursue the study of law at Columbia or Harvard, and from the record that he has made at Georgia it may be safely predicted that his road to the pinnacle of success will be effectively cleared of all obstacles.

name: THOMAS BURDELL PHINIZY, B.S.
motto: "Be serious, giving dignity due respect; confining pleasure to its proper time and place."
description: This worthy son of fair Augusta has been burning with two devouring passions since the mystic veil that hides the wonders and beauties of science, learning, and art from the eager eye of the uninitiated was first dissolved for him by our University. The first of these aspirations—music—has often caused him to turn night into day, and sleepers wrathfully out of bed in search of an old shoe at the chill quiet hour of four A. M. by the sonorous notes of his violin. Tom's second line of endeavor has been mathematics. After a long and exhaustive study he has succeeded in proving false the age-old axiom—"A straight line is the shortest path between two points." He brought to light this startling fact by discovering it is shorter to go from Athens to Augusta by way of Greenwood, S. C., than by the direct route. Tom has made practical use of his discovery on many occasions. Seriously, we have much we would like to say about you, Tom, much that could not be said of everyone. Throughout the years that we have known you we have found you a true friend and a gentleman. We can depend on you. Wherever life may call you we know that there is one thing you will give—your best! Success to you, Tom, and a finer success than prosperity.

name: DANIEL MARSHALL POLLOCK
motto: "What's worth doing at all is worth doing well."
description: An old philosopher made the statement that a certain personage of the past was "not a man of inches, but every inch a man"; which commentary may be applied aptly to our friend and mutual acquaintance, Marshall Pollock. "Polly" is a student who confines his interests not to his books alone, but combines a bit of the practical, and a bit of the social, with his intellectual pursuits in order to attain that "Utopia" which he justly terms "a happy medium." "Polly" has often remarked to his friends that, even though a man conquer the world, he is yet, if he be unable to understand the psychology of a woman's heart, as ignorant of the practical affairs of life as is the most illiterate South Sea Islander. From this statement, my dear reader, you may properly draw conclusions as to "Polly" and his attainments with the "fair sex"—observing, attractive, fascinating, and above all, persevering in his tactics. Marshall Pollock is a true and loyal friend—one who believes that in order to have friends one must be a friend. What greater compliment could be paid to any man?

name: EVELYN POWELL
motto: "Then blow it east or blow it west The wind that blows, that wind is best."
description: Now here is one who would not glitter as tinsel—whose dislike of flattery, whose lack of selfishness, whose love of moderation would prohibit the "trinket lustre" of worldly show. Yet genial, big-souled, optimistic "Ev" has not concealed that sterling worth of untarnished character within her. Friendly to all about her, kind, and considerate of others, she has left her impress upon the "co-ed" life of "Ole Georgia." In the rush and whirl of a busy age such a calm spirit is a rarity; and in a commercial age such love of culture and literature just for the "sake of living" is a gift. So we predict for "Ev" a happy future growing out of her happy present—and we might paint a picture of her laughing face in the home of a "future professor." About one who desires service rather than fame we had better say nothing else lest we thrust greatness upon her.

name: ETHEL PURCELL
motto: "Not that you won or lost—but how you played the game."
description: If you are looking for a conscientious worker, here she is! Although she has been with us only one year she has made for herself a very creditable record scholastically. She possesses a very quiet, gentle, and dignified reserve on first acquaintance, yet she is altogether alert and it requires no "syllogistic" device to prove that she is capable of reflective thought and reasoning. You find her always busy, poster-making being her chief pastime, yet she is never too busy to help a fellow student, especially in Math, this being her "long suit." Besides being a "math shark" she is a speed demon in getting up work. Such perseverity, together with sincerity and absolute dependability lead to success in any line undertaken.

name: RUTH RAE
motto: "Tho' tangled and twisted the course of true love This ditty explains No tangle's so tangled it cannot improve If the lover has brains."
description: To use a familiar expression Ruth is a "knock-out!" We cannot help but believe that Georgia will suffer a great loss when Ruth leaves her sheltering wing. Although Ruth seems partial to a very slight degree to the Sigma Chi's, she has always been the best of "buddies" with the brightest smile for everyone. While the words of her mouth and the meditations of her heart are not always generally known, they are always acceptable in our sight, and we are ready to give her the "hurrah" any day.

name: GEORGE HORACE RIDGWAY
motto: "There are two sides to every street."
description: Among the vast throng of freshmen who came to the University in 1918, a certain young lad was to be noticed for his marked ability to "get by" with things. The most casual observer could readily detect that this man was destined to become one of the best "mixers" in the class. And they were not mistaken for "Montague" in his career at the University has known and made many more real friends than the average student. If, as Mr. Peabody put it, "Education is numbered by the number of frictionless points of contact with the greatest number of persons," then we may truly say that "Montague" is an educated youth. He is what may really be called a "regular fellow." During his stay at Georgia he has applied himself diligently to his work, and his success attained here is a fair index to what it will be when he gets out of school. "Montague," may you always keep and develop that winning disposition, for if you do, the world will be yours.

name: ANGELYN SASNETT
motto: "The world is so full of a number of things, I'm sure we should all be as happy as kings."
description: Anne is so indifferent that she can keep a secret. Of course this does not sound reasonable, but who wants to be reasonable when a woman is concerned? After breaking in Y. W. C. A. Cabinet, House Government, Pioneer, Zodiac, and a few other organizations, Anne very calmly decided that the greatest achievement of them all was to look like she never heard of them, hence the frivolous air. Don't let it fool you—she knows enough to keep it a secret, which after all is a greater honor than a ring of Phi Beta Kappa keys.

name: MARION DAVIS SEABORN, Jr.
motto: "The worst of failures is the man who fails because he can't think fast enough."
description: Graduating from Boys' High in 1919 Davis decided to try his collegiate fortune at the University. For four years he has been a familiar figure on the campus, trotting in and out of the library with huge piles of books, and ever seeking someone to assist him to support Pop. Unlike most of us, he has succeeded in finding a place in which to while away the evenings, and several times during the week he can be seen boarding a car as if he were going to visit someone. We are set to wondering. He seems to have conceived a strange desire to wander about a bit, and we should not be surprised to encounter him some day among the South Sea Isles placidly smoking a Chesterfield and absorbedly perusing his inevitable Saturday Evening Post. He is not so foolish as to be satisfied with most conditions, nor does he succumb to all the rigors of current convention.

name: CAROLYN SIMS
motto: "Pleasure fills our youthful years, Drop study if it interferes."
description: After three years at the Normal School, Bib came to Georgia, full of "pep" 'n everything. Her few years of confinement did nothing towards stunting her growth in the line of attractions! She has only to whistle and the whole and entire Delta house is at her feet. Were I to enumerate her many virtues I would be writing till doomsday, but such an epistle is not necessary, for she has radiated about her such a prism of sunshine and personality that even "Goats" cannot resist the magnetism. We are proud of her and we are sure that some day, ages hence, someone will be saying, "Bib? Yep—Bib from Georgia!"

name: MARY ELIZABETH SIMS
motto: "We've joys and sorrows every day, So 'grin and bear it', come what may."
description: If the earth were turning over, I'd turn to Mary. If Salvation depended on me, I'd depend on Mary. If something must be done, And I must select the one Who'd dot it well for fun, I'd select Mary. Who gives the world her best? Why Mary— Whose truth has stood the test? Why Mary's— Whose heart is ever bound In the friendships she has found? Who's our very "best all-around"? Why Mary.

name: JOSEPH LeCONTE TALLEY
enrolltime: two first years of his college training
description: Talley decided in high school that he needed a little higher "prep training, so he spent one of his high school years at Mercer. Realizing at the end of the year its total inadequacy, he made a still worse mistake and spent two years at Georgia Tech. At last he found the error his way and came to the University for the two first years of his college training. Talley has been seriously handicapped since his arrival here on account of the fact that he spent his first year in our midst at the Y. M. C. A. He has overcome, however, all these almost insurmountable obstacles, and has made Old College a "regular" inmate. He is a man of many accomplishments—a dreamer, who has not made dreams his master; a Ford mechanic who knows Chaucer and how to run a hog farm; and on top of it all he is red-headed. Can one imagine a more heterogeneous individual? They say he frequents as often as the matrons will permit, a certain young ladies' institution down at Milledgeville. And we can see his finish. But no matter whether he travels in double or single harness he will get there just the same. We're for him.

name: EDWIN THOMAS, A.B.
motto: "I have no philosophy of life. What is it all about anyway?"
enrolltime: In the short period of three years that it has taken Edwin to attain his degree
clubs: Student Instructor in the Department of Romance Languages
description: Here is the "Champion Alternate of the University!" In the short period of three years that it has taken Edwin to attain his degree he has won more alternate's places than has any other man in Georgia's long history. Imbued with a certain degree of versatility he has made an enviable record at the University. He has stood for three years among the very highest in scholarship, and besides has found time to participate in the varied branches of college life. His success at Georgia might be attributed to his apparent motto, "Ne cherchez pas la femme." If success is for those who work, he has already succeeded. He departs from the University leaving a record to be copied—a versatile worker and a friend worth having.

name: ROBERT BRUCE TWITTY, A.B.
motto: "You'll never find anything unless you are looking for something."
enrolltime: returned to the University last September after an extended vacation of about five years
description: Any of us who complain of getting such a short vacation might envy Bob, who returned to the University last September after an extended vacation of about five years. He was really here during the last summer school, but that was only a fitting climax to his long vacation. Yes, Bob attended the summer school despite the fact that he hates women in general, co-eds in particular, and is a paid-up member of the Diogenesian Club. Bob says living in the city with all its bright lights and pool rooms never has appealed to him. And as soon as he gets his goatskin he will flee to the southern sands of Florida, where he expects to attain fame by crossing the hickory and grapefruit trees, thus obtaining an orange that won't split when one tries to suck it. Perhaps he inherits this trait of persistency from his great ancestor Robert Bruce. Luck to you, Bob!

name: SAMUEL LAMARTINE VARNEDOE, A. B.
description: Gentlemen, we have here in this young Apollo of Savannah a peculiar combination. Not content with possessing the symmetry of form and grace of a Grecian athlete, the eloquence of Demosthenes and sagacity of a Theophrastus, he has of late developed the political ability of Lloyd George and the generalship of Napolean. As a politician and organizer of the "Democratic Party" of "Georgia," Sam was a knock-out—but we will have to hand it to him in passing as being a man who is willing to stand up for his convictions and who will champion any cause he thinks is right, no matter what the odds are in regard to its success. Sam has ever been popular as a tea-hound and athlete. He has mixed in all of the fraternity encounters, and they say in Savannah that he is as much at home on the raging main as he is in a drawing room. If he maintains the same consistency of purpose and determination to do the right thing regardless of results, he will succeed, no matter where he goes. The best of luck to you, Sam!

name: ELKIN VOGT, B.S.
motto: "It takes clothes to make the woman, but all that is necessary to make a man is a derby."
description: Here's to six feet of blonde-haired nonchalance. Elkin Vogt is an alumnus of Bay's High, where he made his mark both as a student and as an athlete, being a short-stop on their base-ball team and a star end in football. At the University he has made an excellent record in all of his work, making Junior Cabinet. He has done such exceptional work in the zoology department that he has been made an assistant. Vogt is quiet, good natured, friendly, a man of the highest ideals, and a knockout with the ladies, though he will not admit it. In spite of a disposition to rove there is without a doubt a great future before this young man. His plan, at present, is to take up the study of medicine. Luck to you, old man. We believe in you and expect to hear from you in the future.

name: JOSEPH HENRY WALKER, JR.
motto: "Charm wins the sight, but merit wins the soul."
description: The crowd was tense with excitement as our "friend to be," "Clay" Walker of Walker Park, Georgia, led the Monroe boys in a rousing cheer for his "prep" team. This was many years ago when he was a lover of all forms of activities which tended to show manhood and poweress; today, hardened by four years of college WORK, he prefers indoor sports and the parlor couch to any form of outdoor avocatin. Our friend "Walk"—we say our friend, for he divides all people into two classes, friends and enemies—is a man of many sterling qualities. He would make an excellent bill collector, a good English student, a fair husband, and a superb "John Alden." J. Henry often mutters to himself, "I may not be good looking, and I may not be brilliant, but I hope some day to make a success of life—didn't Ceasar and Cromwell make their lives immortal under similar handicaps?"

name: MARY OLIVE WHITEHEAD
motto: "Ah, but a man's reach should exceed his grasp, Or, what's a heaven for?"
clubs: Chairman of Publicity Committee Y. W. C. A. '22
description: Poise and grace, a true artistic sense, clearness of thought and bigness of heart are combined in "Molly-O." She is always ready for a frolic and yet is sometimes denied indulgence because the judgment that has obtained for her offices of responsibility, leads her to do unto others as she would have others do unto her. Who can think of "Molly-O" without bringing to mind the nuemerous little surprising, but pleasing, traits that make up her character. A distinct aversion for "hot dogs" is one of the most noticeable of these, but even this may be overcome with patience. Molly-O aspires to the professional world. Heres to you, all success—yet we wonder, can the world be so blind to charm!

name: ALSA BROWN WILLIAMS, JR.
motto: "There can be no truly happy life devoid of work and friends."
description: The University of Georgia can be rightly judged only by referring to the graduates who have passed from under her protecting care. We can honestly say that we are glad to be judged by such a standard as "A.B." Having been in close association with him for four years, we have come to love and respect him, not because of any publicity or small honor notoriety but because by his words and actions he has always shown himself to be a gentleman and proven himself to be a true friend. Fully realizing his purpose in coming to Georgia, his efforts have been directed to the accomplishment of that end, and is needless to say that he has been successful. Now he would not many a fair lady could testify, or as you would vouch for yourself if you could listen to the give you the idea that "A. B." is a book-worm. No, indeed! His is no one-sided nature as sweet music of his violin. In parting, our best wishes accompany you, and may your future success be as real and as satisfying as your life has been within these hallowed walls.

name: DAVID AYRES COLLINGS, JR.
motto: "Strive and see if you can always be a gentleman."
clubs: President of Y. M. C. A.
description: The biographer for this man has a rather difficult task in view of the fact that everyone that has heard of "Georgia" during the past four years has heard of "Dave" Collings. Like Achilles was famous for his heel, "Dave" is famous for his toe,—unlike Achilles, whose weakness was in the back part of his foot. "Dave's" ability with his toe has made him famous. On the football field he has been a wondrous piece of hard-scrapping material. Many are the times when our hero's dependable toe has sent the pigskin hurtling from the shadow of "Georgia's" goal post far back into the enemy's territory. Truly his punting is a wonder to behold. Not only has "Dave" served his Alma Mater faithfully on the Gridiron, but as a student,—well, A. M. after his name testifies to his ability as a student. "Dave" Georgia will certainly miss you when the next scholastic year rolls around.

name: GORDON WORTH CHAMBERS
motto: "It is easier to find fault, than to be faultless."
clubs: President of Henry W. Grady Speaking Club
President of Judicial Order of Advocates
description: Here we have Gordon W. Chambers, legal friend, conversationalist, hard worker, a good student, writer, soldier, leader, and a gentleman of the highest type. One can not mistake his business-like figure as he strolls across the campus. He has hung closely to the Law Building, worked busily, talked fluently, and mastered choice intricacies of the law. "Pick" deserves much praise because in the short space of three years he has risen from being a "target" of Dr. Morris to the very high and exalted position of his "right hand man." "Pick" has no marked peculiarity, except the ability to see far into the future of the realistic and idealistic world. He is not only a scholar, idealist and hard worker, but is an exemplifying type of manhood that our Alma Mater is always proud to call her own.

name: LAWRENCE DOUGLAS COOK
motto: "Give every man your ear, but few thy voice."
description: From an errand boy in the office of the Secretary of State, his father, "Cookie" became a worthy applicant to the noble Justinian Order in the fall of 1919. Unassuming and unforgetful of little kindnesses, he has unconsciously established in the minds of his co-laborers in legal lore his qualities of dependability, honesty, and trustworthiness, as well as an ever present strain of real optimism which says, "Since others have been successful, what reason have I for failure?" Many are the times that the mighty "Sylvie" has challenged the legal strength of our "Cookie," but in each instance he has been thrown back to his third line of defense, and such is the "jurisprudential" make-up of our hero. In statue he wears the boots of the mighty Stevens and we can expect from him, also, achievements worthy of the praise and admiration of the University and the Empire State.

name: THOMAS HAYES COOLEY, LL.B.
motto: "Work, live the truth, let your defeats be your stepping stones, then you are the master of success."
clubs: Vice President of the Junior Law Class
description: Three years ago "Judge," having been reliably informed that a certain "Silvy" was the fountain head of legal knowledge, departed from Elberton with a longing for precepts with which to secure unto his fellow citizens their rights and incidentally win for himself eminence and independence. Suffice it to say that he has acquitted himself excellently in the acquisition of said precepts. His greatest asset is a smile which never leaves his face unless some technicality of the law reduces his mark to 98 or 99, which it is seldom able to do. Companioned by his law book and inevitable smile he looks without envy on all the world. He is the youngest captain in the United States Army. He has selected his partner from the first year law class, and, Judge, we are assured that you will explore the most lofty peaks of your chosen profession.

name: CYRUS WEST FIELDS, LL.B.
motto: "When my dog and mother die, my friends are gone,"
description: "Cy" besides being a good all round fellow and student is a veritable sponge of information. Anything that pertains to the records of the world's affairs or the legal works are his specialties. He has a faculty for legal absorption, and the three years that he has spent here have developed him very fully on the jurisprudence of the United States and of Great Britain. "Cy" is a good companion and you would not find a better fellow if you were to search for years. He is one of the most popular men in the Law School. Converse! If he has a fault it is neglecting to do something for a conversation. He is a hard worker and has a determination to succeed. He is ambitious to achieve the highest judicial position in the gift of the people, and if he shows the spirit as a graduate that he has exhibited here, he will sit with honor on the highest throne of confidence.

name: GEORGE GRIFFIN FINCH, LL.B.
motto: "Think less of your trouble and how you got into it, and more how to get out of it."
enrolltime: Coming to us three years ago
description: This is to inform the few who do not know the distinguished record of this gentleman from Fulton County. Coming to us three years ago from the army, not a meek freshman, he has ascended to the ethereal heights of a noble scholar. His averages have been the despair of competitors. "G-Square" is a smasher of "idols," a scorner of the sensual, always against the vulgar, he has stuck the gaffles of truth deep into the sides of wrong, and exposed crookedness wherever he found it. "G-Square" is a man to write about, requiring more space than the jealous editors will grant, but we will say that simple faith is his armor plate, honesty his shield, and truth his sword. Burning words came from his tongue, scorching and branding every fraud.

name: HARVEY DIXON GRIFFIN, LL.B.
motto: "To make bad things good, and good things better."
enrolltime: during his five years at Georgia
description: While he has made no unusual record during his five years at Georgia, Harvey is perhaps one of the best all-round men in college. Being a good student, a good boxer, a good singer, a good track man, a good pool shot, or in brief a good fellow in general, his activities have been many and he has done well in them all. Now unlike the majority of the class we do not predict for Harvey a brilliant future. He is one of the few among us who is not destined to be great. In all probability he will be a "jack leg" lawyer down in Baxley, Georgia, where the people have already started to calling him colonel. There he will marry, settle down, raise a large family, prosper in a small way, and then die. But we'll bet that he will always have the friendship and respect of his neighbors, as he has done up here. And what is greater than that?

name: ERNEST JOHN HAAR, LL.B.
motto: "Knowledge is better than weapons of war."
description: All hail the boys from Savannah—, and among our best appears Earnest John Haar. E. J. came to the University in the fall of 1920, after having completed his prep career at Georgia Military Academy. His genial personality and ready smiles have won for him many friends and wherever known he is genuinely liked. Though not exactly an A-Plus student, he has delivered the goods. We feel confident that if he can convince a jury as easily as he can obtain an affirmative answer from the fair sex, his success is assured. A lion in deliberation, a lamb in personal dealings, as firm as the mountain base, as persistent as the ocean waves that come ceaselessly to the jagged cliffs, that's "E. J." These and other descriptions; still you haven't heard the half of it. Good luck to you E. J.

name: ERNEST CLIFFORD HOGAN, LL.B.
motto: "Do as adversaries do in law—strive mightily, but eat and drink together as friends."
description: Behold the "Ex-Senator from the 'Old Ninth'" G. M. of Campbell County. He won this appellation by his successful debates while in high school, and that same argumentative disposition has remained with him throughout his college career. At all times he seeks to convince others that his views are correct, and he usually does it, for one generally concedes he is right after hearing him argue any point. His success in convincing others lies in the fact that he is always careful to choose the right side of a controversy. It is predicted that he will continue this practice as a lawyer and become the most formidable advocate of Campbell County's illustrious sons. Let the girls of Fayetteville, Ga., take notice of this fact.

name: EDWARD YOUNG HOWARD, LL.B.
motto: "A good judge decides fairly, preferring equity to strict law."
description: An inspection of Ned's honors shows that he has been thoroughly inbred with the importance of legal matters. This examination, continued still further, reveals in his philosophy of life an even greater saturation with law. These observations show Ned's likes are as they should be, for after his graduation in June, his intention is to practice law—in Stephens, presumably. A write-up for this gentleman, "Ned," "Uncle Bim", or "E. Y.", as he is variedly known by his many friends, seems hardly necessary—he is too noteworthy a figure to be so summarily considered. Withal, it seems unfair to be compelled to write the hackneyed expression "Mr. Howard is a perfect gentleman." His demeanor proves his gentlemanliness much better than we can ever hope to do by mere words.

name: ROY SEXTON JONES, LL.B.
description: Time was, when young romantic lovers wooed and won their ladies by singing 'neath their windows. It is a good thing that Roy didn't live in those days for methinks there would have been much need for laws against polygamy! His indifference toward the fair sex has saved many a broken heart! Roy is the exemplar of American youth. Reckless, genial, carefree and democratic, and yet with a spirit that never says die. In appearance a young god, with all the gifts a god can bestow, and yet with a great lovable nature that makes him just Roy. He doesn't take himself seriously. He doesn't take life seriously. He lavishes his gifts freely, and offers his hand to all. An athlete, musician, speaker, and actor—and yet he makes light of his own accomplishments. There is no ego in Roy. Yet few people know the real Roy. He has ideals and ambitions which he cherishes and follows with unusual sincerity and perseverance. A host of staunch friends are expecting him to be a scintillating star in the legal world of the coming generation.

name: HAROLD KASSEWITZ
motto: "Our whole life is like a play."
description: Kassy entered the University admittedly the best speaker from among the High Schools of the State. Disdaining the superficial honors which gravitate to men who distinguish themselves in forensic circles, he gave his attention to more profitable fields of endeavor. Unquestionably the best actor in college, he employed his talent to become not the President, but the Business Manager of the Thalians. As a result, the Thalians enjoyed the most successful year in their history—and the most profitable. Quite casually he devoted a bit of his otherwise leisure time to the study of law, with this result—as in all of his activities Kassy came out the big winner. He will easily top his class unless one among the "students" in Law School slip by him by a tenth of a tenth of a point.

name: CHARLES ARTHUR LEWIS
motto: "I see the right and I approve it too,— Condemn the wrong, and yet the wrong pursue."
clubs: General Sec. Y. M. C. A.
description: 'Tis recorded that back in the ages about 1915 A. D. the sages of Terrell prophesied that in 1923 great things should come to pass, because in that year "graveyard" would come forth from such institutions of preparation as the U. of Ga., West Point, and the Y. M. C. A., with both natural and artificial laws at his command. His first intention was to go to West Point and prepare to occupy the office of commander-in-chief of the U. S. Army. While there, however, he learned he would have to be a second "Lieu" as a condition-precedent, so he changed the scene of preparation from the banks of the Hudson to the banks of the Oconee and the pursuit of knowledge from natural laws to artificial ones. He has since been directing his energy in various activities as his unusual quota of college honors will evidence.

name: William Howard Mewbourne
motto: "Live and let live."
description: "Sailor" came to the University two years ago as a graduate of the University of Virginia. Prior to his school days at Virginia the "Sailor" was a student for a spell at Mercer. School life in these various institutions has given this man a broad viewpoint which is noticed by his associates in his every action. His record here speaks for itself. Within a few weeks after his entrance in the University of Georgia his delightful personality had pierced practically every man on the campus. Immediately he begun to take part in almost every student activity until today he is a man to be figured in every organization. When we say that the "Sailor" is a man, we mean a man in the full sense of the word. His untiring energy together with his strength of character are sure to lead him to success.

name: John Walton McWhirter
motto: "Wisdom has often sought me, But I scorned the love she brought me, My only books have been woman's looks, And folly is all she has taught me."
description: "Mac", our honored president, is truly a man born to honor wed. There is something about this steady, conscientious, hard-working individual which commands the love and respect of his fellow creatures—such a man is "Mac", as we affectionately know him. Although, taking law, and hailing from Royston, the two greatest handicaps that could be placed on a Freshman entering this grand old University, "Mac" has shown himself "fit to fight" and has surmounted these and other obstacles that have been thrown in his pathway until now he finds himself ready to fly away from the top rung of the ladder of collegiate legal attainment to make his way in Georgia's Temples of Justice.

name: HOWARD POOL, LL.B.
motto: "Truth is as strong as death; jealousy is as cruel as the grave."
description: The Dacula, Georgia R. F. D. carrier has lost a good customer, and a good southern cotton picker has been ruined, but, so be it, the legal profession will profit thereby. "H" came to us in the fall of '19 from the lecture platform, after having many times held the unlettered minds in gaping awe as words of wisdom trickled from his immortal lips. After completing one year of academic work he heard the call for better lawyers and became a disciple of "Sylvie." Besides being a consistent student "H" has developed into a smooth politician. If we were to predict, we would mark his destinies unparalleled in noble achievements. A leader of men, a thorough and consistent worker, confident in his ability, we are compelled to predict for him a brilliant career in the practice of his chosen profession.

name: JAMES RALPH ROSSER, LL.B.
motto: "If we are right we do not dread criticism from others."
clubs: Vice-President of the Senior Law Class
description: To "Ted", Walker County can well commit her reputation. He pledged his affiliations in the study of law for three years, and has bravely marched down the battle-alley, under the fire of the batteries of "Sylvie"; surviving, but battle scarred. As a student, he has earned commendation. Consistent in study, loyal in interest, and able in preparedness; he is rewarded in scholarship, political achievement, literary and social prominence. For "Georgia", in athletics, he has played the game like a true sportsman, if downed, never out. From the press, we contemplate consulting "Rosser on Injustice." In truth, we see "Ted", a man conscientious, painstaking, and loyal to duty; and when our college days are but memories, we look to see your ambition materialize, your name linked with eminence, and your life a monument.

name: Charles Morton Strahan
description: Dean of the School of Engineering. C. and M. E., Sc.D.

name: WILLIAM FLOYD ABERCROMBIE
motto: "When you find yourself in the wrong, turn to the right."
description: Someone has said "Doctors can bury their mistakes, but an Engineer's mistakes will bury him." Ab is an Engineer who started "engineering" in Douglasville several years ago. He has been here with us four years now, and has convinced us that we need have no fear for his safety in the game of life. Although being "crowded" with visits to Moore College he has been a leader in the Military line. We can't tell you just what "Ab" is planning to do in the years to come, but he will probably be putting some of little Charlie's theory into practice. Well, folks, we could continue to write and tell you more of this gentleman's good qualities, but those who know him are already aware of these, and those who don't know him probably won't read this, so we will just say in a few words, a better fellow can't be found! Here's to you and yours, Ab, may you live long and prosper.

name: AARON BUSH
motto: "I'll add my mite in making the world a better place in which to live."
description: In 1919 A. Bush entered the University with the keen desire to make an electrical engineer. With a view of continuing his course elsewhere, he has taken a large amount of work not required in his prescribed course. He has taken four years of two subjects—Physics and Mathematics. Honors do not worry him, but, if one can come to him in a perfectly fair manner, it finds a warm reception. If unfair means have to be employed, he doesn't need them. In short, he believes that fairness and frankness are very good attributes of any student. As an ardent radio "bug", his efforts were crowned with success when, on December 12, 1922, his station, 4 FB, was heard in England during the Trans-Atlantic Tests. It is practically certain that this is just the beginning of a long series of wonderful achievements by him. Bush, you have your Alma Mater back of you!

name: HARRY EARL ELDRIDGE
motto: "Build for the future, but live in the present."
description: "Old Timer" originally hailed from the vicinity of Ringgold, Georgia, but, travel and experience have branded him as a man of the world at large. After helping Uncle Sam put the skids under the Kaiser, Eldridge came to Georgia and has proved himself to be the last word in versatility. He is a good Engineer, a snappy military man and a ball player of no mean ability. Aside from these diversions "Old Timer" is vitally interested in one whom he calls the "Duchess." On more occasions than one, "Old Timer's" timely bingles have saved the Bulldogs from defeat and set the grandstand wild with joy. Throughout it all "Old Timer's" head and neck have remained the same size, and because of his talents, achievements and personality he is the idol of the red-blooded followers of the Red and Black.

name: CHARLES HENRY NEWTON, Jr.
motto: "It's not what you wish for, but what you work for."
description: We all know this good-natured six footer and we all like him. Charlie is an Athens boy and proud of it. He is a graduate of Athens High, where he was President of the Senior Class of 1919. He is another of Professor Straham's engineers and a good one, too. He is clean, pleasant, good natured, of the highest ideals, and one of the best all-round fellows in college. We will have to admit, however, that Charlie is rather strong for the ladies. He intends to engineer west where we expect him to leave his stakes in things worth while. Here's hoping you don't get too far off, Charlie, for though your fame may travel back, all the great things you could do would not take the place with your many friends that your sincere personality has had. Luck to you, Charlie, old man.

name: JAMES ANDERSON NEWTON, B.S C.E.
motto: "Let your opinion of others be tempered with time and justice."
description: "Fig" got his ancestry balled up and thinking himself a descendant of that illustrious gentleman who invented gravitation and that he might have inherited some of the old gent's mathematical mind, decided to study civil engineering. Regardless of who his forefathers might have been, Sir Isaac Newton, to the contrary notwithstanding, "Fig" showed his stuff when he made Sine and Tangent, the worthy desideratum of all engineers. The number of a man's friends is in proportion to the number of his nicknames. "Fig" answers "Hey O" to "Whitey", "Fig", "J. A.", "Cottontop", and "Sir Isaac". All these bespeak his likable personality. If his opinion of anyone is not the best, he keeps his mouth shut about it. He rarely says what he thinks; usually only what he knows, but that's not why his tongue doesn't get "overhet." If "Sir Isaac" makes as able an engineer as he is a good sport, some day we'll be flivvering to Europe over concrete and dry dirt. "Fig" says: "Say it with figures, for figures never lie". Figuratively speaking, then "Fig", we hope that your future will be as happy as your hair is white.

name: WILLIAM HUGH YOUNG, B.S.C.E.
motto: "Let none falter who thinks he is right."
description: Hugh is a man whose ability as a student and as a man of affairs is never questioned. He hails from the small, but well-known town of Cartersville, which he condescends to admit is somewhat smaller than Atlanta. Pleasing and affectionate in his personality, he impresses his associates as a man distinctly worth while. It would be difficult to find a man with a keener sense of duty, a more resolute determination, or a more sparkling intellect. During his three-year sojourn at the University he has accomplished much in every activity with which he has associated himself. We honor Hugh for his ability, seek his sage council, and admire him for his sterling qualities of character. It would be nothing less than a reflection of our pseudo-prophetical ability should we predict anything less than a place of leadership in life's hectic struggle for him. Accept our best wishes for every success.

name: ULYSSES VASCO STANCILL, LL.B.
motto: "I choose the course I think is right and press forward regardless of what people say or think."
description: Stancill came silently out of the mountains and has remained silently with us, characterized by the simplicity and rugged honesty of North Georgia's granite hills. In a straight-forward manner, never varying a hair's breadth from his conceptions of right, he has fought his way very creditably through four years of college. He is a strange mixture of obstinacy, determination, and perseverance; a man seemingly forever serious, yet with a keen sense of humor; good, but far from ecclesiastic. He has created his own opportunities in life, and has used them well. His story is a book well begun; may the remaining chapters follow in climactic order!

name: GEORGE HARDY TREADWELL, LL.B.
motto: "Smile and soon another smile, and soon there are miles and miles of smiles."
description: "Crepe" after traveling all over the country decided that Georgia was the place of his choice. He paid Georgia a three-year visit, finding "Silvie" Morris his host. Did you enjoy your visit? Someone asked. He replied, "Well—you can argue pro and con." You wouldn't exactly call this young gentleman a woman hater—for he is in a perfect love triangle. He seems to be against the saying "Love triangles end up in wrecked angles." A grand prize has been offered to the one who catches George not telling a new joke, strange to say his new jokes possess a peculiar ability for breaking up "bull sessions." George is the kind of a man we all admire. He possesses the qualities which constitutes a real gentleman. His many friends are wondering if he intends to make money off other people's "troubles." In whatever you undertake George we wish you success. We will miss you. Good luck to you "Crepe."

name: Glenn Cheatham Tyson
motto: "Things without remedy should be without regard; what's done is done."
description: Bill is only half "Geechee" for he comes from Darien, if you know where that is. You'll recognize it if you drive up so that the telephone pole won't obstruct your view. Bill is a veteran of the S. A. T. C. days, and he says Athens is a better place to live in than Darien. You wouldn't believe it, but Bill is in love, and when June comes we're all hoping to have the pleasure of congratulating him twice—once for his "dip" and once for "Sid." With his disposition a man could win any girl. He is a bit quiet at times, but the "Flying Squadron" has nothing on him when he is "not quiet." "Judge," your future is as bright as the sun, and we know you'll represent us in Washington before many moons. Keep up the good fight, and the best of luck to you.

name: John Ernest Wilcox
motto: "It is better to be, than to seem to be."
description: Ladies and Gentlemen, before you smiles the beaming countenance of one of the leaders of the Senior Law Class of the Lumpkin Law School. In 1917, when Uncle Sam blew his trumpet, "Judge" was one of the four millions who answered it, and as a result, his legal education was a trifle delayed. While a bit eccentric, Judge can always be counted on to "hold the knowledge of law", which gave rise to the saying, "when in doubt, ask Judge Wilcox!" Since the beginning of the year, "Judge" has had the benefit of an excellent guardian, in McWhirter, self-appointed. And under "Mac's" able guidance we look for "Judge" to some day occupy a seat on the Supreme Court of these old United States. McWhirter is strong on this "Me and You" stuff with the Judge. It being big me (McWhirter) and little you ("Judge").

name: ENOCH HOOD WILKERSON, LL.B.
motto: "One who is certain without knowing achieves the maximum of uncertainity."
clubs: President of the Jeffersonian Debating Society
description: Fresh from the inferno of the Argonne, a battle scarred veteran of the famous "Rainbow" Division, and haunted by his envious dreams of "Russell" and "Taft", "Squire's" ambition was fired to study law. His first ideal materialized, when, robbing the Bible of all its love he won unto himself a helpmate. Determined, he mounted the task, visualizing a student's duty, he utilized the opportunity, and meritoriously, has he won. Once 75, and oh! how disgraced! Never again below 98. Truth reveals the real man. He is an honor man in scholarship; a leader of men; a patient and untiring adventurer into the broad fields of knowledge; his is a high standard of character; devoted to truth, honesty, reliability; and a devout observer of the precept—"justice to all." Looking on Wilkerson today, our prophecies are fulfilled, as he climbs to his envious dreams, and from the summit his life ploughs a noble furrow in his country's fallow fields.

name: Andrew MacNair Soule
major: College of Agriculture and Mechanics Arts
clubs: President of College of Agriculture and Mechanics Arts

name: OMER CLYDE ADERHOLD
motto: "A defensive life is like a defensive team—never a success."
description: This quiet and congenial student calls Lavonia his home, at least that is where his "Pa" hauls his guano from. It was a sad day in this young man's life when he had to leave old "Huldy" and the plow behind to pursue his studies at the Agricultural College. "O. C." has for four years been one of the leading men in his class, having made a very high scholastic average, as well as taking an active part in the major college activities. Aderhold has the record of not filling a single date at the "Co-Ed Barn" during his sojourn at the University. He considers this a commendable record. "O. C." performs all of his work in a conscientious manner believing that, "anything that is worth doing, is worth doing well." Gentlemen, he is a real man, and will make his influence for good felt in any field of endeavor that he may choose.

name: CLIFFORD WARD BAKER
motto: "The attainment of success demands either genius or hard work."
description: "Wyll" Baker is the pride of Candler Hall, the joy of the co-ed Barn and the terror of New College. While others were wildly rushing and scrambling for meaningless college honors and marks, Baker contented himself with making friends, doing a moderate amount of studying, chasing co-eds occasionally, and attending his club meetings regularly. Baker's popularity has been attributed to his intimate association with "Tub" McLendon. No doubt his rotund colleague had something to do with his popularity but his participation in various college activities has been the potent factor in making him one of the most popular men on the campus. Baker's ambition has always been to shoot "Little Jack." He has accomplished this extraordinary feat two or three times during his college career. This is a record to be proud of when "Little Jack's" invulnerability is given due consideration. Baker, your profession expects much of you.

name: JOHN LEE BOLTON, B.S.A.
motto: "Better not be at all than not be noble."
description: In the fall of 1920 John came to Georgia for a course in Agriculture. By dint of hard work and diligent application he has completed the full degree in three years. At the same time he has done sufficiently well in his class work to place him in the upper fifth of his class and into Alpha Zeta. He has not been exceptionally active in any line other than scholarship, although, he has, at times, tried his luck in the social world. The results of his efforts along the lines of social endeavor would cause many a professed teahound to blush with shame. In fact, John has an unusual drag with the fair sex which many of his associates envy. However, coming down to brass tacks once more, John has made good at Georgia and by his worth of character has made many friends. We can truthfully say that we believe that success will come to John, if, in the future he continues the stride he has maintained during his stay at Georgia.

name: ROBERT LEE BIGGS, D.V.M.
description: Bob served Uncle Sam in the Veterinary Corps during the war, and while thus engaged he got his incentive to study Veterinary Medicine. Instead of going back to the wilds of Oconee County, he came to the University and joined that band, intent upon learning the science which deals with the prevention and alleviation of the sufferings of our domestic animals. Biggs joined the ranks of the "benedicts" a few years ago and since his appearance in double harness he has developed into a dynamo of energy. Besides keeping up with his regular college work, Bob has built up a dairying business near Athens, of which he is justly proud. We do not know what line of work that Bob will follow, but we do know that his everlasting grit, his willingness to work and his determination to win will attribute considerably toward his success.

name: David Frank Bruce
motto: "Be sure you are right and then go ahead."
clubs: President of Saddle and Sirloin Club '22
description: David Frank is a native of the Classic City, immigrating originally from South Georgia. He is a hard worker and believes that asking plenty of questions goes a long way toward convincing the Profs that he is interested. He has done much in the way of putting the Saddle and Sirloin Club on the map, and the Epworths into the Methodist Church. He is a Military man of considerable note, having risen to the rank of Major of the Cavalry Unit. He rides with all the dash of Tom Mix and handles troops in a manner worthy of Napoleon. Bruce has already cut his wisdom teeth and is getting slightly bald, however, we predict for him a long and eventful life as a County Agent.

name: Sydney Grigsby Chandler
motto: "Contentment is death to ambition."
description: This West Virginia boy has made a huge success of his stay at Georgia. He knows the true value of things, and is not often led astray on matters of secondary importance. "Syd" is extremely versatile in his accomplishments, as he can shingle a house, handle a troop, confound the women, or make high marks with ease and felicity. At the same time he makes sure to get enough sleep and is careful not to overwork himself, since he believes that a mark achievement should be judged not by the effort he puts out but by the results he gets. We haven't room to tell you of all of Syd's manly qualities, but we are willing to shout from the housetops that he is every inch a man. Knowing him as we do, we feel sure that he will make his mark in the world as he has done here.

name: FLEMING DUBIGNION CHASTAIN, B.S.A.
motto: "It matters not what you are, but what other people think you are."
description: "The world loves a fighter." The one who wrote those words either had Chastain in mind or else he had visualized his coming. His stalwart body, his undying zeal, his hard fighting, his loyalty and his strong character denote him as a man worthy of the name. "Chas" belongs to that group of athletes whose names never appeared in the headlines, but whose presence on Sanford Field had a great deal to do with the building of Georgia's varsity squads. It takes a man to play scrub football during his Junior and Senior years, but "Chas" fills the bill. Chastain stepped into the political limelight this year and the Agricultural Club rewarded his efforts by electing him its President for the third term. Luck to you Chastain. If you continue to fight as you have been fighting, yours will be a rich reward.

name: I. P. COULTER, D.V.M.
motto: "He who speaks without thinking is a fool; he who thinks without speaking is a coward."
clubs: Business Manager of the Georgia Agriculturist
description: "Phreely" Coulter is something entirely different. "Phreely" registered from the State at Large four years ago and took the noted "Crip" course of Veterinary Medicine. He has been erroneously called "Bolshevik" merely because he asserts that he does not believe in law and order. He has, however, been more correctly called "Co-ed Hater." His political influence has been felt on several occasions although, he never sought but one office for himself. He was elected Business Manager of the Georgia Agriculturist, which he piloted through a critical period of its history. Coulter is one man who does not number his friends by his acquaintances and is proud of the fact for he believes that one should naturally make some enemies if he is of a steady character. He calls a spade a spade and gets away with it. His motto, selected by himself, is a good index to his character. If he follows this motto he will succeed.

name: ELISHA KETRON DAVIS, B.S.A.
motto: "Do what you know and you'll always know what to do."
description: He came, he saw, he conquered the stenographers and co-eds. "Ket" is an energetic young fellow who knows the many wiles of the filthy medium of exchange in this noble land of prohibition and the I. W. W. He is a quiet, unassuming, and a very likable fellow who has memorized every scientific word in Agriculture and is one of the leaders of his class. "Ket" is one of Candler Hall's four year men, a man that Candler Hall is proud of because for those high ideas that Candler Hall represents, such as "Prohibition," To H-ll with New College," etc. Although with a natural weakness for the ladies he eclipses this minor tendency with his higher ambitions for doing big things and we shall not be surprised when we hear of his promoting large enterprises in Vocational Agriculture work. The best of luck to you, "Ket."

name: NAPOLEON VANCE DAVIS, B.S.A.
motto: "To succeed you must be willing to pay the price of success, which is work and more work."
description: "Nap" has been accused of breaking more school ma'ams' hearts during the past three summer schools than any other student. However, this is no serious charge since he is specializing in poultry and chickens. But an accurate survey shows that Napoleon is a serious minded man and that he has made a notable record at the University. He is one of the many who gave three of the best years of his life to his country. This only served to create in him a greater desire for knowledge. In the fall of 1919 he came to an ideal institution from the town of Ideal and has made an ideal student, having completed two years of high school work and a four year college course in less than four years. We believe in you, "Nap," and feel assured that in the future you will be as loyal to your country as you were to the principles of democracy.

name: HENRY WATSON DERDEN
motto: "A majority is not necessarily always right."
description: Mr. Derden might well be called "The Man Without A Country" since he comes from Ellijay, Georgia. His college career has been a success in every way. He has not "boot-licked" or "politicked" for anything, but has entered into community-activities as a whole. Each year of Henry's life has been rich in girl-lore. Especially is this true of his Junior year, when his visits to Whitehall were very frequent. Normal School authorities say that he has a natural bent for flirting; it may be true. He and "One" of the authorities came very near to having serious trouble, but they never happened to cross each others path. Mr. Derden is self-supporting and always optimistic about life. His pleasing personality makes him scores of friends and he is bound to make good at any undertaking. Some girl surely has a great treat in store.

name: WILLMARTH WINANS DRAKE
motto: "Inward happiness is a source of constant pleasure; but all the pleasures of the world cannot make one happy."
description: In all classes of endeavor there are always some few men who set the pace for the rest. Such a man is W. W. Drake. Having lost one year, he entered this class in 1920, bringing terror to the hearts of the would-be honor seekers. For he is a shark, gobbling up what other men must ponder over, he makes hard things seem easy. We are glad that he does not let his work interfere with his outside activities. He has plenty of time to cultivate friendships, and is identified with many important clubs. Drake is a good man to know and his keen brain is sure to bring him success. Luck to you old man, may you always average "90."

name: COLUMBUS JOSEPH DURHAM, D.V.M.
motto: "Always make one believe you know, else you display your ignorance."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Senior Class
description: Joe came to the University in the fall of '19, along with that host of green recruits.
