He has had many difficulties to overcome while here but with that courage and determination for which he is noted he has overcome all of these. At the mention of his name, to the minds of those who have known him here, comes the thought of faithfulness and courage. 'Joe' has spent the large part of his time in the study of Veterinary Medicine of which he has acquired a most excellent knowledge. As sidelines he has acquainted himself somewhat with several of the stringed instruments. 'Joe' has also wielded a masterful hand with the ladies on many occasions. With his knowledge of Veterinary Science, general adaptation to all surroundings and that big wholesome laugh at all times 'Joe' could not fail no matter where he is nor what he is doing.

name: ERASTUS LAMAR ENGLAND, B.S.A.
motto: "Circumstances determine ones destiny 'tis true, but the person usually makes the circumstances."
description: "Rastus" got restive way up in his mountain home, Blairsville, and decided that he would come away. He has been in Athens for four years and has distinguished himself in many ways. His is the cheeriest smiles on the campus. He debates with the power of Huxley, pulls political strings with the deftness of Watson and adds his friends with a Burroughs Machine. England learned in his days of teaching that grades aren't the only thing, so here he has devoted himself to many activities which have netted him several political offices and a vast front covered with pins and medals. We are proud of his acquaintance and feel sure his Colonial Sessions will be extensive ere he is old. "Rule Brittania."

name: ARTHUR FRANCIS GANNON, B.S.A.
motto: "Make up your mind as to what you want to do—then do it."
description: In Arthur we have the connecting link between the student body and the faculty, for he is not only a student but also an instructor in Poultry Husbandry. He does not limit his interests to the feathered fowls, but he is besides a connoisseur of "chickens" in the broadest and most charming sense. Although he is a "geechee" he has accomplished much during his four years at the University, and gives promise to much more in the future. He is a man of few words and many actions, who believes that it is proper to think twice before speaking once; but when once aroused he is as fluent as a Clay and as brilliant as a Webster. In Savannah he goes under the name of Francis, at the University he goes by the name of Arthur among the boys, and "Artie" among the Co-Eds; but in his classroom he is better known by his subservient pupils as Dr. Gannon or "Fess."

name: JOHN WYNN GILLESPIE, B.S.A.
motto: "They can because they think they can."
description: John came to Georgia to study Agriculture, hailing from Albany, the city noted for Y. M. C. A. teams and Wesleyan girls. Aside from doing everything in a scholastic way, he has yet found time to play much tennis and read many books. He is a horticulturist, but because he has devoted quite a bit of time to pecans does not mean that he is in any way "nutty." This year he rose to the rank of Professor, taking a position in the Botany Department. He really did four years' work in three, but having been out a term he is graduating with his class, having done considerable work on his master's degree. We are in doubt as to whether he will go back to Albany and raise a family of seven boys or take a chair at Cornell.

name: HOMER JEFFERSON HARDIN, B. S. A.
motto: "He is truly wise who profits by the mistakes of others."
description: Now gentle reader, do not get excited. Hawk is not the "cake-eater" that he looks to be, so do not expect any wild tales such as are common to "Ruddie." The only wild thing he ever did was on a steamer going to Boston. He was immune to feminine charms until then. Since that summer he has been passing all his courses, and receiving very thick letters daily. We hope that she will like the quiet life down on the farm. "Hawk" has been with us four years, but he does not raise as much noise around the house as most of the boys. Still water runs deep, and with this in mind, and knowing Hawk's perseverance and determination we look forward into the future to seeing him one of Georgia's most successful farmers. His quiet way, his steady character, and his eagerness to learn will surely bring him success. It's been a pleasure, "Hawk," to know you; may you always have good luck.

name: WILLIAM JOEL HART, B. S. A.
motto: "Laughter Labor and Love are the Cardinal Qualities."
clubs: Cadet First Lieutenant and Adjutant M. T. C. Cadets
First Student Awarded Joe Brown Connally Scholarship
description: This man lives up to his life's philosophy. To pick "Bill" is like picking one out of ten thousand. He is justly termed a student, a gentleman and a man. One who contends that silence is elegant. Says little, but does much, acting according to his insight which accounts for his efforts being crowned with success. He is very reserved in placing himself foremost in anything, therefore, intimacy is required to appreciate his real value and sterling character. It is a wise man who knows his business, but a wiser one who attends to it. "Bill" has the rare honor of being in the category of both. No finer type of real manhood can be found. "Every true friend is a glimpse of God",—Hart is a friend to all. Success to you "Bill" in whatever you undertake.

name: ALLEN HENRY HASTY, B. S. A.
motto: "Let neither time nor effort stand between you and accomplishment."
description: This big hearted gentleman, a farmer by inheritance and an Agronomist by vocation, will probably some day startle the world by crossing a cotton plant with a rag weed and producing a plant that will make cloth of the highest quality. "A. H." is primarily a student of Agriculture and has done well. He is a genial, well-tempered man and will make some community a good citizen. From his career here any of us might receive an inspiration, while he has made few honors, in the broad sense of the word, he has made all honors. As a man he has worked his way through High School and College, coming out with a clean record and a head full of good common sense. He has a bright future and is a man that will let neither time nor effort stand between him and accomplishment. We trust that you will ever work for the high ideals you have set, "A. H.", and may success ever smile upon you.

name: CHARLES STUART HODGES, Jr., B.S.A.
motto: "Let me live in a house by the side of the road and be a friend to man."
description: Who is that young fellow running around second base so lively? That is Charlie Hodges, and he is as happy as he looks to be. That is true, Charlie is known to be a lover of the game, and an enthusiastic fan in any sport in which his Alma Mater participates. For the first three years Charlie ran around quite a bit, giving the dances a rush and entering eagerly into any college activity, but lately his friends have noticed that he has quieted down considerably. Boys there is a reason. Ask "Puss" Whelchel what happened in summer school, and there you will find the answer. Charlie numbers his friends by his acquaintances, indeed it is said that he hasn't an enemy in the world, and all join in wishing him a long life filled with happiness, peace and success.

name: JOHN WESLEY HOWARD
motto: "Weave steadily and with clear intention at thy design, knowing full well the value of secrecy."
description: John is a good example of what continued effort will do when applied along any line. He did not suddenly flash out as a great fellow, but gradually gained his high standing in college circles. His most outstanding efforts have been on the cinder path, where he was just an ordinary man to start with, but by continued training he has become an outstanding figure. Everyone admires him for his never-ending courage, not only in track work, but in class work, and college activities as well. Besides, being a good student and an athlete, he is a successful politician. He has put into practice some of the knowledge gained while here and thereby bettered his financial standing. Thru all his work John carries a smile; this same smile and his ever-training to meet all problems will surely make him an exceptional citizen in the future.

name: OSCAR LEON JOHNSON
motto: "Effort, energy, and enthusiasm is the price of success. Every day in every way I put forth greater effort, more energy and enthusiasm."
description: "O. L." came to us three years ago from Jeff Davis County and his record shows that he has lived up to his motto. Johnson, when a mere boy, conceived of one thought, that of improving the rural school of Georgia. The larger portion of his life has been devoted to that idea. Within three years he has completed a four-year college course and at the same time earned his expenses. His is a unique record, inasmuch as he is the oldest student in the University, the first man from his county to graduate from this institution, and one of the few who has had no political aspirations nor envied those who made college honors. Oscar, you have confidence in your ability; you are willing to pay the price for success and being prepared to render worthy service to humanity; we believe that you will make the world better by having lived in it.

name: HENRY GROSS KICKLIGHTER, B.S.A.
motto: "After every thing is said and done, tighten your belt and never run,—but be a man."
enrolltime: Class of '19, Glennville High; entered with the toughest and oldest Freshman class in the University's history
description: "Little Kick", as the above is known among the Co-Eds, is a native of the "Marshes of Glennville, and a shining star of the Class of '19, Glennville High, plus being a whole bunch of comets in his native heath. After thinking seriously over the possibilities of man-kind "Kick" decided that to be a Georgia alumnus would be one of the greatest achievements of his life. "Kick" entered with the toughest and oldest Freshman class in the University's history, and many were the hardships wreaked on him by the vigilance committee while he was weaning himself from the harmonious singing of Tattnall County's champion bullfrog. Since "Kick" has been with us he has won the reputation of being a man of splendid morals and unreproachable conduct. His love for truth and his infinite patience is the stairway by which he will mount to higher things. Stay in there and fight 'em boy! We are for you.

name: LUCIUS ROSSER LANGLEY, B. S. A.
motto: "What counts in life is not so much what you are as what you are becoming."
description: "Phi Mu" is truly a man of the soil. As that is his cherished field he will some day give to man many of the hidden treasures lying therein, and elevate the noble profession even higher than it is today. Berry School turned loose a green Freshman, but Georgia has converted him into a finished product. We admire a man who will stand up for what he thinks is right and fight for it. Naturally, Langley is a more or less of a ladies man, but does not let his admiration along that line interfere with his studies. He has a host of friends and to know him better is to appreciate his friendship more. Langley is quiet and modest, a good student, and a good friend. He has done well and we believe the future holds much worth while for him. We wish you the best of luck, old fellow.

name: THURSTON LEVIE, D.V.M.
motto: "The more you see of others the less you settle to one."
description: Levie is one of the few survivors of the S. A. T. C. campaign which was fought and won on Herty Field in the fall of 1918. After the smoke of the battle cleared, Levie went back to High School to absorb more knowledge before entering upon his regular college career. The worst blot to be found on Levie's record is the fact that he roomed with Joe Durham for two years. Despite this handicap, Levie is a regular fellow and can actually converse without arguing. Levie is the best horseshoe tosser in college. This is quite an honor when all the local talent is given consideration. He does not lay claim to being a devil with the women, but this adds to, rather than detracts from, his virtues. Laying all foolishness aside, Levie is the kind of fellow whom we all admire. He is quiet, thoughtful and friendly. As he is one of the best students in the Veterinary Department, we predict for him a great future in the practice of his profession.

name: WALTER AUBREY LUNDY, B.S.A.
motto: "The ear that heareth the reproof of life abideth among the wise."
clubs: First Lieutenant Cadet Corps M. T. C.
description: Since Lundy has been among us he has proved to be so retiring that few know him, as you would judge by the bunch of honors listed above. In fact, his is such a retiring nature that he is hardly known outside of Clarke County. However, he will get over his backwardness, we hope, and in a year or so settle down to grow pecans as a vocation and chickens as an avocation. Foolishness aside, Lundy has proved to be so popular and so good a politician that his class elected him as its president. That shows how he is fixed when it comes to resourcefulness and initiative. It is useless to say he attributes it all to being red-headed. Anyway we are expecting him to push that old red bean of his up near the top, "being as how" the old fight that wins is there.

name: HERBERT HENRY MADDOX
motto: "Be Brief."
description: It was the 5th District A. & M. that sent us this handsome, dreamy-eyed specimen in the fall of '19. In his Freshman year he won for himself that nickname "Sleepy", as well as the reputation of being a diligent student. In later years he has distinguished himself in other realms of activity, salesmanship, politics, and society. Never has he been known to be tardy at drill more than five times a week. "Sleepy" is, as a member of the fair species expressed it, a "perfect gentleman." He has an unlimited amount of perseverance, and a disposition which wins for him a host of friends wherever he goes. We predict for him unbounded success in whatever field of endeavor he may enter.

name: GEORGE MACK MILLER
description: Nearly every one has heard of "woman haters", especially since the Co-Eds were admitted to the University, but never has there been a man who deserved the name more than this young gentleman. In Waycross he has absolutely nothing to do with them; in Athens, he has still less. His time is spent largely in taking notes on Jarnigan's bull, reading Captain Billy's literature, and other things equally as profitable. As a member of the Georgia Naturalists, we feel safe in saying that he knows as much about nature, especially the swamps, as any man in the club, for he has devoted a large amount of his time to this. Moreover he has lived on the edge of the Okefeenokee for the past twenty-seven years. Why not give him the name of "Swamp Lizzard" instead of "Goat" for many of us feel that this name would be more appropriate. We feel, however, that no one can and does follow anything more honorable than the study of nature and that Ware County will do well to have this young man return there for his life's work. May success be yours "Goat", for your many friends will be pulling for you in all your undertakings.

name: M. D. MOBLEY
description: Following in the wake of the World War there hailed from the metropolis of Dallas, Georgia, this lad with a desire for greater knowledge. With a splendid prep school record "M. D." came to the University in the fall of '19. His policy is to seek no honor for honor's sake, but to work and be patient and let honors come as a reward of merit. Since his arrival at Georgia Mobley has demonstrated his ability as a student by completing the requirements for his B.S.A. degree in three and one-half years. During the same period he has conducted a successful courtship, and on November 5th, 1921, took unto himself a wife. Blessed indeed is the woman who has that privilege, for M. D. makes a model husband. With the ability to do, and a companion to encourage him, we predict for him a most successful career in his chosen profession. Luck to you, Mobley, we are for you.

name: JOSEPH WORLEY MOON
motto: "Go as far as you can, then see how far you can go."
description: Ladies and Gentlemen, let me introduce you to a man who stayed at the University three years, never owed "Nick", never went into the pool room until two weeks before he left college, and never cut a class. He is one of the few, if not the only man, that ever entered the University with no advanced credits and received a B.S.A. degree in three years without attending a summer school. No harder worker and no keener student ever came to the old school than this lad from Bowman. He came to college a few years later in life than the average student, and came for the purpose of securing a degree in agriculture. To this end he applied himself unreservedly. Had he entered other fields of student activities he no doubt would have been equally successful. No man ever left college with a truer bunch of friends than did Worley Moon. To know him is to like him.

name: JAMES PAUL MURPHEY, B.S.A.
motto: "Do not allow anything to stand in the way of success that can be honorably removed."
description: September, four years ago, when the Chancellor went down to open up the gate to the Freshman Class who should he find but "Shorty" Murphey trying to scale the wall, and shouting, "Let me at your hard courses. I'll ruin 'em." And ruin 'em he did. There have been times according to his own confession, when "Shorty" was financially hanging on by the skin of his teeth. However, a little thing like beginning a School year with only five bucks in the till meant nothing in his young life. As to manipulating a Ford with a spray barrel up behind, well, right there "Shorty" has proved himself to be a whole school of sharks. During the past summer "Shorty" rated as one of the boll weevil experts sent out by the city of Athens. This should be proof-positive that he knows his business.

name: EDGAR ARLO NESMITH, B.S.A.
motto: "All good has for its foundation clean principles, and in order to be clean in principles one must be pure in thought."
description: "Steamboat" hailed from Statesboro, Georgia, in 1918, and spent one year in diligent study. The following June he joined the Bible agent's army and for three months plodded the "Angels' Dust Trail." On account of the economical depression which came that summer Nesmith was left with more Bibles than he had time to read. The only profit that he got was the experience of sleeping under hay stacks and an increased vocabulary of slang. Therefore, Arlo accepted a position as a teacher in one of the Bulloch County rural schools; but having such an irresistible desire to become a "big bean", he found himself in the fall of 1921 back again in the halls of learning. During his second sojourn he has won many friends and has utilized his time to good advantage. He is one of the few that is completing a four-year course in three years. Arlo is a man of strong convictions and an equally strong determination. We predict success for you, Nesmith. In whatever field of work you enter we bid you God-speed.

name: Young Marion Patterson
motto: "It matters not if I won the game, but it matters how I won it."
description: "Pat", the mystery man, is without a doubt the most consistant visitor of the Co-Ed Barn of which the Senior Class can boast. It is also known that he has given the Honorable Dean "Richie" no small amount of trouble since his appearance here. Although it has been two years since "Pat" got his Sophomore English off, he is still a good friend of "Bob" Park and continues to frequent his office, being on "Bob's" honorable-proctor-pay-poll. Notwithstanding the above short-comings "Pat" is one of the hardest and most consistant workers that has ever registered at the Agricultural College. He came here from the 9th District A. & M. School where he distinguished himself as a worker and a man that has the determination necessary to see a proposition through. We predict for "Pat" a great future.

name: Walter Lee Patterson
motto: "The only way to have friends is to be one."
enrolltime: the first fall after the war
description: Well, well, gentlemen, we come at last to a subject worthy of our discussion. "Big Pat" entered the University the first fall after the war and has attended classes given in this institution more or less regularly ever since. He has been fairly successful in satisfying the demands of the faculty as his presence in this volume of Pandora will testify. His main hobby, however, has been the Military Department where he has risen from the lowly rank of a sergeant to the astounding heights of a majority. "Big Pat" is a sound fellow in every respect and 'tho he has never strived for the highest record in scholarship he has made a good average grade. However, he has some little weaknesses. The most evident of these is his irresistible desire to invest in balloon stock to the extreme annoyance of the balloon magnates. Another is his liking for members of the opposite sex where his downy pate makes him very popular. "Big Pat" has ability and energy, and with these two characteristics he should make good.

name: JEPTHA P. SANDERS
motto: "The greatest happiness that can come to any man is a conscious pursuit of a worthy purpose."
description: There came a time in the autumnal days of that eventful year when a stranger was seen silently and morosely wending his way thru the flotsam and jetsam of raucous youth to the portals of the inner shrine—the main room of the academic building. The purpose of this wanderer was to become affiliated without further ado with the grand high mogul of the powers that be. A lesson was to be learned. Innumerable yards of multi-colored tape must be unhooked, and the candidate measured for the open sesame. Breasting the outwash of that formidable occasion seems but the scampering of elfish hoodlums in comparison to the hellish slings of outrageous fortune, as dosed out by the benignant caretakers of the official papyri. But take heart, kind friend, and wish with me that "sealing night may come and scarf up the tender eye of the pitiful day." The gentleman whose portrait is herewith attached has been with us three short years, but that has been sufficient time for him to insatiate himself into the hearts of his fellow students.

name: DONALD DEWEY SCARBOROUGH
motto: "Not the number of hours worked, but the quality of the finished product gauges a man's true worth."
description: Donald Dewey claims Marietta as his home town, but admits that he doesn't live right "slap up in town, only twelve mile out." By what means he was coerced into coming to college no one knows, but it seems that college life or some other influence has broadened him considerably, as he now wears a No 7 3-4 hat and shoes of corresponding magnitude. Besides getting his B.S.A., and M.S.A. in four years and annexing most of the honors in sight he has found time to do concentrated and extensive work in other lines. Quiet and modest, but self-reliant and thorough, he usually puts through anything that he attempts. Good luck to you old man and may you have the future you deserve.

name: BYRON LESTER SOUTHWELL
motto: “Wherever you are, be considerate, truthful and industrious.”
clubs: President of the Saddle and Sirloin Club
description: From the land of the swamp mullet and the Geechee comes this enterprising young man, and he has been in the midst of things ever since he first favored us with his appearance in 1919. A consistant student, he is one of the kind which in the mad rush for honors is contented in simply making friends. If any one has any more friends than "B. L." we would like to meet him, for although there are others with possibly more acquaintances few of them have as many true friends, and none of them could be a better one. He has been above the average in his studies, and his extra time has been well spent in outside activities. Whether he runs a stock farm or teaches school, we could predict for him nothing other than an unqualified success, for in him are embodied all the virtues of a gentleman and all the prerequisites for a career of constructive leadership. May you always succeed, "B. L.", to the same degree that you have while we have known you here. We could wish you nothing better.

name: ADDISON KNOX SWIFT
motto: “Life is a sporting proposition after all—the more you play, the more you work.”
description: Behold the blithe and trusting Mr. Swift, plucked from the sturdy yokelry of equatorial South Georgia, as gentle as the mild zephyrs that fanned his hair on his native heath, as scrupulous as the day is long and friend of the brotherhood of man. "Speedy" need feel no compunctions of conscience as he folds his tent and carries the genius of his agricultural hand to the scene of his future application. He has done his work well; his personality has been a paragon for his companions to respect, and there is recorded no instance wherein he scorned an opportunity to lend a helping hand. Oh! genial Addison, that your feeble biographer possessed the rich store of friendship you have founded on your brilliant character! Here was a man evolved for us; a wondrous philosophy—character is the first attainment. We believe that this golden-haired lad, a crusader oft-times to the shrine of Fair Goddess Fortune, will mould for himself an entirely desirable niche within the ranks of his stolid citizenry.

name: FRED ANDREW TANNER, B.S.A.
motto: "Temperance is the total abstinence from all things which are harmful, and the moderate use only of those which are not."
description: "Goat" or "Goat from the Gap", as he is known to every Georgia student, is a man that has no insignificant part in convincing the fairer sex that all the stronger sex are deceivers, liars and heart-breakers. "Goat" does various things of which only a few can be mentioned. On returning from the "Gap" he "Butts" all the glass out of Candler's front door, drives his head into the cement sidewalk, visits the Co-ed Barn with unparalleled regularity, and by some unknown means finds out the price of sugar in Gainesville, Georgia. "Goat" has made a wonderful record since coming here. He has represented Georgia nobly in many phases of college activities, especially on the gridiron, being one of the greatest defensive backs that the South has ever produced. The "Gap" should feel proud that this "Goat" got through, and should receive him back with all bars lowered.

name: HOWARD OTIS THOMAS, B.S.A.
motto: "Always keep your best foot in front, and never worry."
description: Let me introduce to you a man that has taken his "grub" at the "Beanery" for four years and yet has never known how it feels to be there for breakfast; has drilled four years and yet "cut" half the classes; has passed all his work and never "cracked" a book; has "bootlicked" more professors than any other student in college; has been interested in politics the whole four years and has never held but one office, namely, Treasurer of the Ag. Club; has been one of the large contributors to "Nick's" stand, and last, but by no means least, has suffered the agonies of a disappointed "Mountain" love affair. He is a man that has stood the test and has not been found wanting. "Mutt" is a man of ability and a man that can meet the world as he finds it. There is no use to wish him good luck—that has always been his.

name: WALLACE WILLIAM THOMAS, B.S.A.
motto: "It is not how well you have done, but what you have done compared with what you might have done that counts."
description: "W. W." entered the University four years ago to further his studies along agricultural lines which he started in High School. Just as he arrived on the campus the Sophs presented him with a haircut, and a few nights later he gave them an exhibition dance in return, on his study table (at their request). His work and walks to the Ag. Hill have won for him the long looked for sheep skin. You never go to his room and find him idle, and this trait alone is enough to convince us that he will make good in the business world. Furthermore he will be an asset to any business with which he is affiliated. "W. W." we all hate to see you go, but being equipped as you are, we can rest assured that you will come out on top. Here's to you, old man, hit 'em as hard in the future as you have in the past, and you'll get by.

name: GEORGE AMOS TURNER, B S.A.
motto: "With love and inflexible determination you will reach the goal."
description: George drove a team of oxen from Ball Ground to college, and finding things different here, he sold them for enough money to pay one year's expenses. This same ingenuity has been with him all through his college career; he has no trouble in making his way and keeping up with his studies at the same time. Unlike some others who have had much work to do, he has found ample time for social development and has put that time to good use. Without George the Engineering Department would be lost, and "Profain" would not know who to depend upon. Sometimes, however, they do get tired of hunting for monkey wrenches down there and later finding them in George's pockets, where he has been carrying them around to help people in fixing Fords and lawn mowers. George has made a consistent fight; we can all profit by his example of setting a goal and working toward it. Good fortune be with you always!

name: MARION PETER USRY, B.S.A.
description: Motorcycle Pete! How many members of the "aggravating" sex have thrilled at the mere whisper of the name—how many members of his rivals have shudderingly sought the elongated timber when he cast his gauntlet into the ring. Pete and his motorcycle are absolutely a double-dipped combination cross between a stick of dynamite and a roll of barb wire. Now fair reader don't get the hallucination fixed too firmly that our Pete is forever blowing the gilded bubbles of pleasure. No, he has found time in his four-year stay with us to play with his fellowmen, study some, and "politick" a great deal. In his play he has made friends; in his "politicking" he has successfully run more than one dark bay mare. And thru study he has gained this place in the annual—no small thing to do when you consider that Pete picked no "crips"—B.S.A. after his name is evidence of this. Luck to you, Pete!

name: THOMAS AUGUSTUS WHITENER, B. S. A.
motto: "He who follows the crowd should not be followed far."
enrolltime: three years experience teaching music, swimming, and spooning
description: Whitfield County suffered a great loss, while Clarke County enjoyed an invaluable gain, when the above young lad transferred his place of abode from the former to the latter place. Whitener, or "Handsome Tom," as he was called, is by far the best looker in his class. His wonderful personality, combined with a bass voice that has been unexcelled at the University of Georgia for years, has won for him lasting friendship in the hearts of the students and great popularity in Athens. Tom has had three years experience teaching music, swimming, and spooning, which caused him to make the following statement: "The height of my ambition is to be Dean of Women in some up-to-date female college." He is a good student, of fine character and ability. He has not "bootlicked" anyone, but has stood on his own feet. He is a man in the fullest sense of the word. So we say, "Luck to you Old Boy."

name: Robert Preston Brooks, Ph.D.
major: Dean of the School of Commerce

name: PAUL KEMPER ANDERSON, B. S. C.
motto: "Run not, for the world is round and not an endless plane."
description: "Halt! Who goes there? Pass Colonel Anderson." And so after four years of it "P. K." passes to struggle with the outside. He came to Georgia after fighting for Uncle Sam in France; here he has been fighting on the football field and over numerous bridge and tea-tables. However, he has made an enviable record in his classes and has succeeded in capturing numerous scholastic honors. Those who are close to him love him greatly, wish for him all the success in the world, and believe firmly that he will attain it. Here's our best to you "P. K." May you carry on as gallantly hereafter as you have heretofore.

name: MILLER RAIFORD BELL, B.S.C.
description: This man has been an asset to the University. As a student he has been an example. He has studied hard, and aside from his work he has found time to mix with his fellow-students. We are afraid in writing this epic that we will fail to bring out the many good points of "Buck". Besides being a good student he has been quite a hand with the ladies. He is a man of true ability, practicing, not preaching his convictions; sincere and successful in the attainments of his purposes, and he could not be thought of otherwise than a true gentleman. True to his friends, and just to his adversaries, we foresee in him—not a demagogue of parties, but a pilot of principle. He has faithfully performed his work, and as he goes out of the portals of the University, he carries the best wishes of a host of friends.

name: DANIEL PAGE BENNETT, B.S.C.
motto: "When the One Great Scorer comes to write against your name He writes not whether you've won or lost, but how you played the game."
description: The University inherited from G. M. C. in January 1920 this gentleman, athlete and scholar. "Highpocket" is a man of sterling character always standing for the highest ideal, and putting them into practice in his daily life. To those who know him this need not be said. His determination and spirit have been a great aid in swaying the victory for our grand old Georgia upon many occasions, both on the Gridiron and the Basketball Court. Never shall we forget that Georgia-Auburn game in Columbus in 1921 and the part "Highpocket" played. He is also handsome and can be seen with the ladies when any social function is in order. It has been said that a woman's heart will not last in the sunshine of his smile any longer than the proverbial snowball in H--. "Highpocket" has made the University a good man, and in leaving takes with him the best wishes of his many friends for a prosperous future.

name: ROBERT ALEXANDER BOWEN, B.S.C.
motto: "Don't waste words."
description: After a heated discussion at Chateau-Thierry with the forces of Kaiser Bill, in which he received a few souvenirs of German lead, "Pete" decided he would tackle the hard-boiled professors of the Universitas Georgiae. He therefore entered the Commerce Department in 1919, and has since become involved in many heated discussions with the cohorts of "Pistol" Jenkins. "Pete" has never been pictured by his closest friends as a calculating business man, nor as a wizard of finance, and from all indications his ambitions do not run in that line. Any man who can write his poetry and short stories, and write them with apparently no effort, ought certainly to do so later in life. "Pete" has a whole host of friends, who predict for him a brilliant career. May his victories be many and his wound stripes few in the battle of life. We wish him the best of luck.

name: CLARENCE ELMO BROCKINTON
motto: "Life with its many problems can be solved easier with the consolation that whoever lives true life—loves true love."
description: Here we have, ladies and gentlemen, a man of no mean ability—Mr. C. Elmo Brockinton, who is familiarly known by many names, among which are "Brock", "Beau-Brummel" and "Man-o'-War". The last named is his favorite, however, and is very appropriate. He is our only representative from the frog ponds and marshes of Glynn, but they are well represented—for "Man-o'-War" is some worker and a fast one. He has made good in his work, both in school and out, especially the last. And as his honors indicate he has taken a prominent part in student activities. He is without doubt the most desperate lover in college—therefore a good one. If you don't believe it look at his philosophy of life or ask him. He admits it—although "his mule" has been scared several times. But all in all Brock is a good man and sure to succeed in any business he may have.

name: JOHN FRANKLIN BURT
motto: "Let your conscience be the guiding light of your life."
clubs: charter member of the "Woman Haters Club"
description: Burt started his schooling at Point Peter and finished at Athens High. Point Peter was unheard of until he came into our midst, but during his stay here he has been trying to explain to us where it is. He cannot understand why it is not on the map. He says that they have two stores there and are contemplating building one more. Burt made the famous Old College football team which lost only one game during the entire season. We are sure he would have made the Varsity if he had only gone out. He is a charter member of the "Woman Haters Club", but nevertheless you can see him going down to the Co-op every morning to get that letter from the "only girl." Burt has made an enviable record during his stay at the University and has also made many friends. If you know him you can but like him. We are sure you will make a success. Luck to you "Burt"

name: GEORGE J. CLARK, B.S.C.
description: George is truly one of the strongest men in his class. Carrying the limit in his scholastic work this man graduates with about all of the honor that the old institution can thrust upon him. He is one man that excels in almost every phase of college life. Besides his captaincy of the Baseball team he is also president of the Pan-Hellenic Council—two honors much coveted in college circles. We are safe when we say that this man has hosts of friends on and off the campus. His congenial disposition and his fidelity to principle have functioned in his every action and are daily leading him to success.

name: CLARENCE HENRY COHEN, B.S.C.
clubs: Chief intelligence officer of the R. O. T. C.
description: "Fat" hails from the metropolis of Augusta, and consequently received his early military training at the Academy of Richmond County, which perhaps accounts for the rumor, once broad-casted over the Campus, that he had received the distinction of being the best dressed cadet in the R. O. T. C. This distinction was won because of the smart and nifty appearance he made in his uniform. In spite of this terrible handicap his knowledge of military tactics was so great as to merit him an appointment as the chief intelligence officer of the R. O. T. C. When it comes to "politicking", "Fat" is the "berries". His understanding of the art(?) would make the old political bosses turn green with envy. His success in this field can be attributed to his ability as a "Bull Artist". With all these vices we can still see a great future in store for him, and our last toast to you "Fat" is that your life may be a long and happy one.

name: JEROME DOUGHERTY DAVIS
description: "Tootie's" clothes don't do him justice. His ability as a pool shark, and to pick winning football teams would not suggest such conservative apparel. Maybe that is his strategic method in capturing fish. Conservative, sincere, and strictly a gentleman at all times, he has made many friends. Those who do not know him well say that he will enter the wholesale grocery business, but those who are well acquainted with "Tootie" see him as a successful stock broker in some large city. His deliberation and forethought are unequalled. "Tootie" has not allowed education to interfere with his pleasures, and though he lives in Athens, he has never missed a football trip to Columbus or Atlanta. Here we find him the best of sports. Jerome, may you always continue to be serious in your duties of life and joyful in your pleasures. Our best wishes are behind you, worthy son of Georgia!

name: EDGAR WILLIS DREW
motto: "Hard work and the ability to stick has a reward well worth while."
description: It takes a considerable amount of determination for a man who has trod the cobble stones of many French villages and listened to the growls of the war hounds of the Entente to put aside his feelings of restlessness and settle within these sequestered halls to make himself a broader man and a better citizenry. But the qualities which enable Drew to survive the hardships of a "dough boy's" life have brought him triumphantly through the successive stages of college activity and to the ultimate goal, Commencement. Possessing that inestimable asset of a pleasing personality, he has won the esteem of his fellows, and by diligently applying himself to his work he has demanded the respect of his instructors. Armed with the lessons of Experience and endowed with the gift of our Alma Mater, you now enter upon the final adventure with the best wishes of the class of '23 ringing in your ears.

name: HARRY WILTON EVERETT, B.S.C.
motto: "Bite off more than you can chew, and chew it, Plan more than you can do, and do it."
description: Harry is another one of the Everett boys to finish at Georgia. They say that when he registered for the last lap of his collegiate career, T. W. Reed heaved a two-ton sigh of relief and took an extra-sized whack of "Climax" by way of celebration. You see before you the likeness of a man who in 1919 discarded the blue of his kinsman's navy, and a few days later donned the olive drab of the military department. He was no sooner registered than he plunged ambitiously into politics emerging the following spring with the crown of Sophomore President resting jauntily on his brow. But alas! the call of a sawmill camp was too much for Harry, and he failed to show up the following fall. However, sawmilling could not hold him down, and he came back, after being out one year, and joined the Co-op. Here's luck to you, Harry.

name: THOMAS RUSSELL EVERETT, B.S.C.
motto: "Keep on stirring, the good will rise to the top; don't get excited."
description: "T. R." once made a great mistake, which he has since rectified—he went to Tech two years. However, after two years of study at the worthy school of Technology he invented a motor that will do everything but answer the phone and mind the baby. We therefore contend that his time was not ill-spent. With his motor about perfected and a sufficient knowledge of engineering he came to "Georgia" to get his degree in Commerce, and he has well succeeded, as the heavy type at the top of this page indicates. During the past year "T. R." fell before the darts of Dan Cupid—fell so hard that he proceeded to purchase a marriage license and exclaim fervently "I will!" He is the type that makes an ideal husband, home-loving and hard-working. He and Mrs. Everett are to be congratulated on finding each other. Everett's one great sin has been his close association with "Pistol" Jenkins and the "Beanery", and he has performed this particular operation of graft with exceeding dexterity. May things always come your way Russell as they have since you came here.

name: PIERCE EUGENE GUNBY
motto: "Smile, work, and think, in order that your life may pave a way to nobler destinies"
clubs: Delegate to National Student Volunteer Convention
Secretary of Y. M. C. A.
description: Smile, work and think, are in truth the philosophy of this young fellow's life. That vivacious, irresistible smile and cheerful disposition distinguishes him from the ordinary. Though frail in body "Square" has a spirit undaunted and has overcome difficulties which would discourage most of us. Withstanding a serious physical handicap he has made a record at the University worthy of emulation. He completed the four-year course for his B.S.C. degree and one year law in three years. At the same time he has taken a leading part in the activities of the campus. All in all he is a true friend, a genial companion, a full fledged optimist, and an all-round fine fellow. We predict for him a great future in his chosen profession of law.

name: NATHANIEL J. HARBEN
motto: "Fools may have ambitions, but it is left to wise men with determination for the fulfillment thereof."
clubs: President of Square and Compass Club
description: Attention! Our friend, Nat, with his soldierly bearing came all the way from Bluffdale, Texas, to get an education. Perhaps he did this because his big brother, George, told him that Georgia would start him off in the way that he should go. "Nat" is a demon among the ladies; long hair, bobbed hair, blonde, and brunette are his favorites. He would quietly walk out of Candler Hall almost every night about 8 o'clock to give the fair ones a treat, and would return long after his roommate had gone to dreamland. Excusing this one great fault, "Nat" is a good worker and has made a good record, and is liked by everyone on the campus. His line of bull and common sense made his professors think that he studied. He completed his course at the end of the Summer School in 1922 and went to Columbia University to study law, and we understand he has kept up the standard of Georgia men by making good.

name: JOHN ALTON HOSCH, B. S. C.
motto: "Life is everlasting—to be continued hereafter."
description: No more adequate tribute can be paid this stalwart son of the "Queen City of the Mountains" than this: "By their works ye shall know them." Entering the stately portals of Old Georgia in 1920, Alton began his career of success as an orator by participating in two victorious debates against Demosthenian. Then branching out in other college activities, he made for himself a name long to be remembered and ever to be envied. In fact, there are few honors which were open to him that he did not obtain. Not only has Alton reached the pinnacle of success along literary, scholastic, and military lines, but he has gathered unto himself life-long friends. Judging from his philosophy of life, we see for Alton success in the present, transcended by continued success in the hereafter.

name: SAMUEL ALFRED IDELSON, B. S. C.
description: An altogether placid and confident young man has ambled hither and yon these past three years, flashing a smile here, a conventional slap on the back there, and dispensing sunshine everywhere. So effectually has he amiably mixed with his fellows that we are honored to present Sam Idelson, possessed of all the candour of a jurist, all the charm of a Lochinvar. If it be true that half the advantages of University training lie in the pleasantry and mutual helpfulness of the environment afforded, then we may say that our good-natured contemporary has shone lucidly in the first half. He has been a collegiate atmosphere himself. Not to have known the airy Samuel were truly to argue yourself unknown within this all too brief span we are now closing. A goodly portion of Idelson's time has been spent in the company of the fair sex, within whose ranks the young man is reputed to create no negligible devastation. Studious he has been at times, and playful too, so that his collegiate career has knotted itself into a hopeless tangle of girl, books, and ribaldry.

name: BENJAMIN FRANKLIN JOEL, Jr.
motto: "The greatest happiness that comes to any person is in the conscious pursuit of a worthy cause."
description: We, who in the past three years have become intimately acquainted with Ben know him as a friend and advisor, be it a matter pertaining to the heart, the pocketbook or otherwise. During Ben's University career he has constantly argued with himself as to whether he should rely on his face as a fortune in the movies, or merely utilize it as a marriage application blank. We are certain that with it he can work wonders. Foolishness aside, Ben came to this retreat of the mind a-weary just three short years ago, and as Time has steadily and speedily plucked the leaves from the calendar, he has swiftly and surely brushed aside the debris between him and the coveted piece of ram's hide, so that today, he stands ready to seize this proclamation from under the very eyes of a watchful faculty. What other evidence of ability could the world require? Especially when the fact is considered that our hero has paused long enough between classes and "bull sessions" to throw monkey wrenches into the blood circulating apparatus of some million or two members of the prevaricating sex. We like you Ben; keep the good work going.

name: ROBERT LOWELL LANE
motto: "Let your speech be better than silence, or be silent."
description: "Smooch" lives six miles from Blakely, and we can understand how he reached there—walked of course. But the mystery is how he reached Athens. After he arrived here, however, he made good. How a man with such nicknames as "Smooch" and "Souse" is capable of making "A's" is food for more thought. Week-ends find him at Costa's and the Georgian slinging chocolate milks and a mean shuffle. Although Lane is quiet and lets you do all the talking, he is one of the best known and liked boys in the School of Commerce. He knows more about accounting than Kester himself, and if you need a C. P. A. next year, call Lane. There is no doubt that Lane will make good—everything he does has his whole interest, is exact, and is done right. The big businesses are looking for you "Souse", go at 'em. We'll endorse you.

name: EARNEST ALGERNON LOWE
motto: "Make your stride a steady one."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Student Council
Editor-in-Chief of the Red and Black staff
Chairman of the Y. M. C. A. Publicity Committee
description: This "gent," the pride of Carr's Station, has been as important in University circles as he was in his home town. He began his career in the fall of 1919 when "Freshman and Sophomore" clashes were as frequent as the "bull sessions" of Candler Hall, and almost at once the name of "Rastus" began to circulate among the upper classmen of that day as it does among the Co-Eds of today. At one time Rastus was a Sports scribe for the Augusta and Savannah papers. The various Athletic teams of Georgia were given their share of publicity in these papers so well that he was elected to the Red and Black staff where he reached the top last fall, and we feel safe in saying that his work in this capacity was always above criticism. His acts, and his ambitions, have been such that brand him as a Christian, and as a gentleman, and one whose opinion is always sought regarding the more complicated matters relative to student life. As Georgia Military College feels proud of her son because of his record here, so shall the University feel proud of his record in life.

name: CHESTER ELIJAH McRAE
motto: "Love, honor and be gay."
description: Here he is, the one and only "Oscar from Mt. Vernon", and like the original dweller therein of a Mt. Vernon, he has never told a lie. Oscar likes the University; in fact, he likes it so well that he hasn't missed a Summer School since Tanyard Branch ran by the postoffice. They have come and gone, good students, bad students, and Summer School students, but Oscar goes on forever. His days are spent hatlessly wandering the easy by-ways—ever ready to pause and toss "El toro", ever ready to lend a dime or borrow a dollar, ever ready to come to the succor of a maiden in distress. Ask the Summer School women! As a student, Oscar hasn't set the woods a-fire, but as a work of art, created solely for the Normal School afternooners he has been a howling success. We predict a great future for you in the movies, Oscar.

name: JAMES AUGUSTUS MORGAN, Jr.
motto: "Visualization is a necessary precedent to Materialization."
description: If you have never met a happy-go-lucky chap who laughs at the best as well as the worst, step for a moment and make yourself acquainted with "Jim." If you should try to size him up from the list of honors you might think he had "just been" at the University and that's all. But this is not true as his record in politics and class work shows for itself. "Jim" merely wished to be conspicuous by not having a list of honors by his name as he might have had. He comes to us from the plains of South Georgia and in three years has captured that which it takes most of the fellows to get in four years. We understand from all reports that Morgan is not satisfied with his education received here, extensive as it is, and intends to journey forth to some higher School of Accounting and some day write across the frosted-glass window of his office, where all may read "James A. Morgan, C. P. A."

name: MOSES GUSTAVUS MURRAY, Jr.
motto: "Honesty, hard work, and faith in God will surely be rewarded by success."
clubs: Student Assistant in the School of Commerce
description: Gustavus, better known to the initiated as "Gus", is universally liked because his very nature seems incapable of offending any one. His smile is one that never wears off and he keeps his troubles to himself. Gus has many sterling qualities which will be an inspiration to all those with whom he is associated. His honors are many and are well deserved. He has taken part in every thing that the University offered him, he reached his zenith when he was made Student Assistant in the Department of Commerce. He has succeeded and the University is proud of this blonde-haired boy. Some day we will point to him with great pride and say, "He was my classmate", because he is a man, true blue and a yard wide.

name: Thomas Howard Rivers
motto: "Success is yours, if you have faith, and work."
description: We present for your approval the "Sheik" of Candler Hall, better known as "Skinny." He hails from the little town of Grange, altho he has followed his usual custom of prevaricating, and has given Louisville the honor of being the place of his abode. "Skinny" has been with us four years, and while he has not gained the distinction of being an A-plus man, he is endowed with the art of making friends, which is by far more valuable than a mere A-plus. "Skinny" has but one weakness; he loves the ladies, the long ones, the short ones, the lean ones, the fat ones. Show him a lady and he is lost. As a member of the Candler Hall football team, "Skinny" established a world record as a substitute. "Skinny", you of the sunny smile and pleasant disposition, may good fortune smile upon you always. And we feel sure that it will if that smile is always as hard to rub off as it has been since we've known you.

name: John Frederick Schwalb
description: Here he is, ladies, you can't find a handsomer man if you look through seven states. Lord Chesterfield was not more polished, nor Beau Brummel more gracious than our "Petey", witness, the ladies fall for him. If he were not the sensible fellow that he is we would predict that next year would find him in the studios at Hollywood. But Pete is not going into the movies, no, instead he tells us that he is going into the insurance line, having received the first principles from Gephart. During his four years with us he has proved to be a true friend and a sterling gentleman. Yours is the spirit that never says die, Pete. Give the world your best and success is yours, for you have the determination and ability to achieve it.

name: LUTHER CLYDE STILL, B.S.C.
motto: "A man's friends are his most valuable assets."
description: Luther is the original "Bolshevik" of the military department. During an illustrious career of four years, in which he has attended drill some thirteen odd times and shaved even less, he has acquired recognition as a second Crassus. But despite his long beard and evil eye, "Stotsy" has a record of which he may indeed be proud. He has taken part in more debates than most of us have heard; he was selected for—but just look at his list of honors, ladies and gentlemen, and I need say no more. These and other things show "Stotsy's" popularity as a man and general recognition as an excellent student. Here's to you, Luther, boy; who wants to be a Napoleon, anyway!

name: ALLEN JULIAN STRICKLAND, Jr., B.S.C.
motto: "Work well done makes pleasure more fun."
description: He was the noblest Roman of them all. He came, he saw, he conquered. This to inform those who do not know of the brilliant record of this young man. "Strick" came to us from W. & L. and Emory. After having "prepped" at the above institutions, he decided to cast his lot with us. Few men can boast of the brilliant record "Strick" has made during his brief sojourn here. He has labored, and labored hard as his honors above will show. It is the delight of the weaker sex to have him among them at a dance, bridge or tea, and it is rumored that he has played havoc with many a fair young damsel's heart. Julian, your personality and kind disposition make us love you; continue your good work and all will be well. Accept our best wishes for a successful career.

name: ALLEN DAVID SUMMERS, Jr.
motto: "Do your best. What more can be expected?"
description: "A. D." hails from Conyers, Rockdale County, Georgia. It isn't on the map, but it's there. This fair young gentleman has been at the University for four years and has mastered all the "bosh" as he calls it, that "Puny" Brooks has to offer. The only thing that "A. D." regrets about his college career is that he waited until his Senior year to finish Physics, but why worry about such trivial matters, just so you absorb this important subject before you leave college is sufficient. We must not neglect to tell you that there are two streets on which he wished to make good, viz: Wall Street and Hill Street. Being a master in the financial world, he certainly has the ability and qualifications to make good on Wall Street, and from what we hear circulating among the fair sex he also has the qualities to win on Hill Street.

name: ROBERT LEE TURMAN
motto: "Know thou thine eggs."
description: Lee's easy grace has made itself felt during the three years he has been with us. Ever balanced, well poised, and assured, his artistic cue strokes are delightful to behold. He has always been an asset to the social life of the University, where his composure has been advantageous to him. He is very charming to his friends. Lee has seen fit to finish his course in three years thereby depriving us of his well-known "line." Such a personality could hardly fail to impress an outside world as it has impressed us. His sojourn with us will remain as a fragrant long enduring memory, when he is departed.

name: JOHN HOLLINGSWORTH VANDIVER, B.S.C.
motto: "Don't waste time talking; action's the thing that counts."
description: "Vandy" hails from that section of the state where they take a kid at the age of five and throw him against the ceiling to try his mettle. If he digs in and sticks, they pronounce him "fit to fight", if not, well, he is just out of luck. "Vandy" must have dug in mightily, for he is about the biggest little package of dynamic vitality and determination that ever wore the habiliments of a Georgia pig-skin warrior. "Vandy" has not only made good on the gridiron, but that same old pluck and "never-say-die" spirit that made of him a great football player has carried him through college in three and one-half years, leaving a commendable bunch of marks behind. He also left behind a certain young lady—but that is another story, and we feel certain that Athens will see him many times before he gets his final papers here. Here's to you, "Vandy"! May you ever fight as hard as you have during the past four years.

name: LOUIS SUMNER WINN, B.S.C.
motto: "Be not discouraged in performing any task which you know you can do—the Gods all assemble to help those who help themselves."
description: This henna haired veteran of a thousand parlor engagements came to us in the fall of 1919, and since then has done remarkably well along all lines, socially as well as scholastically. Despite the fact that he is as handsome as Apollo ever dared to be, and was cut out to be a ladies' man, "Red" is a red-blooded woman hater, despises the women, and although many have tried to woo him he has always managed to give them the slip with his favorite phrase, "Get thee behind me Satan." Louis Sumner hails from Statesboro and we know that the home town hasn't been the same since the municipality farmed "Red" out for four years at the University. "Red" is well trained in home economics and is conceded by every one to be an authority on this subject. He also "knows his material" in auditing and accounting, and during his Junior and Senior years has served as official accountant for the Athens Railway and Electric Company. "Red", we predict for you an unqualified success in the future.

name: J. M. WILLIAMSON
description: "Tony" was originally a simple country boy, but alas, who now would even "suspicion" the aroma of hayseed about the person of this curly-headed, "he-vampire." This boy's chief trouble in life is trying to make days into night, in order that he may have more time to sleep. Twenty hours per day is nothing for this champion slumberer. However, when he can manage to stay awake long enough to grace us with his presence, he is indeed a wonder to behold. His lips are like rubies, but we have sufficient reason to believe that most of his beauty comes from the drug store. His rare collection of cosmetics would make an ordinary female artist blush with shame. Two years ago "Tony" went to Europe to study no less a question than Foreign Trade Conditions. On that trip Dame Fortune placed his future better half in his pathway and since that time when he is not in Athens his address is 6030 Sheridan Road, Chicago, Illinois. Yes, we have all met her and wish "Tony" all the luck in the world, trusting all the while that his life in double harness will not be beset with vicissitudes and too many little troubles.

name: CHARLES RUPERT YOUNGBLOOD
description: "Buddie" resigned his position as president of the Georgia & Florida Railroad and came over to Athens for a little vacation, choosing the School of Commerce for his playground. Now that he is a Senior he has again resumed his work and has taken over the managership of the Commercial Bank. In this field he is doing remarkably well and is fast taking unto himself a number of friends that need a little financing. It is easy to predict the future for a man like this for we feel safe when we say that he will succeed at almost anything that he undertakes. So long as we live we shall never forget the man that has sold more tickets to football games in one year than all the other managers have been able to sell during their entire career. When football games are forgotten we shall never forget the cross-eyed man with the genial personality.

name: Robert Cumming Wilson, Ph.G.
description: Director of the School of Pharmacy

name: Hanna Betrus Miskoff
motto: “Labor, Cheerfulness, and Charity are the Ideals from which life is fitted for happiness.”
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Senior Pharmacy Class
description: Born under the Turkish Crescent in Mesopotamia—that land of little light and few opportunities—Hanna took advantage of the meager facilities for education offered by American Missionaries until the war with its horrors of Turkish massacre closed the schools and put a premium on the life of every Christian. For five years, Hanna roamed the Mesopotamian plains, a camp-trailer to the fighting armies. On the entrance of American relief workers after the armistice Hanna was enrolled as a worker and by his untiring energy and ability to learn, rose from the position of office boy to be chief interpreter and assistant to the Post Surgeon. Coming to America in the spring of 1921 by his own energy and his own efforts, he enrolled in the Pharmacy Department of the University, and, working under perhaps as great a handicap as any student, he has made his way to graduation. As busy as he has necessarily been, he has found time to devote his energies ardently to Y. M. C. A. and Church work. The future success of Hanna Miskoff is written, whether he seek it in the country of his birth or in the country of his adoption.

name: James David Melton
motto: “Be silent or say something better than silence.”
description: We now present for your approval, Gentlemen, the leader of the Pharmacy Class, and very deserving is he of that distinction. "Pete", a native of the Palmetto State, landed in the "Classic City" in September 1921 to become one of us. He has so many good points to draw from with which to write a pleasing description that it is difficult to choose. First of all he is a fine fellow, happy, amiable, and with a smile that won't wear off. He is a good student, and is very popular with those members of the faculty with whom he comes in contact. Besides his ambition to exceed in the art of Pharmacy, he intends to go deeper in the pursuit of a B.S and then into medicine. Luck to you "Pete," and may the best be yours!

name: RUFUS LESTER NEVILLE, Ph.G.
description: Behold, ye who will! This shining article is none other than Mr. Rufus Lester Carbuncions Ambitious Neville of Statesboro and the Pharmacy Department. In two years Neville has completed the requirements for his diploma in pharmacy, plus the stiff pre-medical course. Why shouldn't he be Dr. Wilson's pet? With all that behind him he has a perfect right to walk "kind of" leaning back and wear an overgrown hat band. But Lester does nothing of the kind, on the contrary, he is one of the best "mixers" that ever graced these famous haunts of democratic men. With him "'twas ever thus". If Lester never summers on the sunny shores of fair Sicily, if he never autumns amid the grandiloquent beauties of the Adirondacks, if he never winters where the zephyr-like breezes of the ocean kiss the perpetual green of the palm, if he never springs among the lazy pleasures of southern France, he will still have seen life. For Mr. Neville once attended summer school at the University of Georgia. And looking back on that summer school he can say truthfully, "I have had experience". Lester contemplates attending a medical college next year where we confidently expect him to make good. Luck to you, Lester.

name: FRANCIS ABDA STOVALL, Ph.G.
motto: Always be true to the best that is in you.
description: Finishing his home town High School with a grand flush, Stovy entered the University three years ago and has been true to the cause since that time. Never brilliant, but always consistent, he has done well during his stay with us. Stovy has many hobbies of which Chemistry is the chief one. For the last two years he has amused himself delving into the "Theory of Ionization." In passing let us not forget to say that he has received his share of pink envelopes from members of the lazy sex. Stovy started out with Science, but "Doc" Wilson finally "got him", and converted him into a pill roller of the highest order. Over in Terrell Hall, he may be seen at most anytime overcoming the general art of "compounding." In conclusion; with his good natured smile and pleasing way, Stovy has made himself well liked by those that know him. "Always true to the best that is in him" he has the good wishes of '23.

name: Eunice Bell Youmans
motto: "Our ideals are our better selves."
description: Well, the best way we can spell her name is P-E-A-C-H! That's just the best we can say of anyone. And when we apply that name to one it has a world of meaning. Eunice was with us in '20-'21 and we feared we had lost her, as she failed to show up for the next session. Enough to say, however, that she couldn't stay away long--so she returned to finish this year. Can you imagine such a demure little lady concocting some detailed prescription in a drug store? Far better is she suited to the job of concocting dainty dishes in some nice little kitchen, and it is rumored that she is rather good at prescriptions for "heart trouble." At any rate we wish her the jolliest of luck, wherever she be, whether it be meting out drugs or food supplies--here's to you Eunice, and may yours be the best ever.

name: OMAH VAUGHN BADCOCK
motto: "Take time to smile—it won't be wasted."
description: A jolly disposition, that allows no dull moments, a sweetness of manner, that captivates at once, and a quiet generosity that is felt but not seen, make "O-Me" the companionable girl that she is. Small thoughtful acts that rarely occur to others, with friends for her quite frequently. As a sideline she likes many people and many things; not the least of these things being "movies", looks, and dill pickles. It will seem strange indeed to imagine the ol' campus, streets and "hot dog" stands without "O-Me." Here's to you Omah! May you always receive as much joy and sunshine from life as you give!

name: EMMIE NELSON
motto: "Be true to your word, your work, and your friend."
clubs: Secretary-Treasurer of Student Government Association for Women '22-'23
description: Was ever a girl more ready for fun and frolic than Emmie? She is never too busy to play a prank or to do a favor. The unexpected always springs from her lips and fills her audience with laughter, but her fun is wholesome and wins for her many friends. Emmie was somewhat of a bacteriology shark and made a justle for "98" in spite of her popularity with the opposite sex. Her sunny disposition and winning personality have been a source of inspiration to students and faculty and she will be missed on the campus next year. We can't think of nothing but success for her in any line of future activity that she may choose. The sincerest wishes of her classmates are hers.

name: LILLIAN SHEHEE
motto: "O let the solid ground not fail beneath my feet, Before my life has found what some have found so sweet."
description: Great was the loss of "G.N.I." when Lillian abandoned that school and came to us two years ago, but--well why should we grumble--we're the winners! Lillian followed her natural good judgment and continued her course in "home-ec", for which, as rumor has it, she'll have adequate use--perhaps, real soon. To many, she may give the impression of being somewhat aloof and indifferent, but when it comes to a smile she's right there with the goods. And when you know her, surely you'll love her. A generous nature, an ever-readiness to lend a helping hand, and a sympathetic spirit, combine to make her attractive to the extreme. Here's to you Lillian; may you never tire of delicious concoctions which have so often satisfied our appetites!

name: ADRA LOIS STILLMAN
motto: "What is to be, will be, and what, is, is right."
description: Some people saunter thru life, some run and others dash madly. Lois belongs to the last mentioned category. A few people have been known to go into hysterics for fear Lois would not get to class on time; street car conductors always halt an extra minute at the Co-Ed Barn Stop, and the pedestrians who are wise step aside when "friend Lois" ambles by. But why quibble over how a person does a thing--the point is, she gets there just the same.

name: Wood, Lucy Ann
clubs: B. S. H. E. Soph. Representative Student Government Association for Women '21
B. S. H. E. Junior and Senior Representative Student Government Ass'n '22, '23
description: No truer pioneer has gone out from our great institution than Lucy. Seizing the opportunity to make her life more useful to her fellowman she came to "Georgia" in September, 1919, and has been an enthusiastic participant in the affairs contributing to the progress of co-education. In her steadiness of purpose and untiring devotion to the task before her she has proven that the training which makes women happiest in themselves also makes them most serviceable to others. Her popularity has come largely from her good nature and her willingness to do anyone a favor. She will certainly put any vocational High School on the map, or Home Demonstration work, in any county, in the papers, because she can cook good enough to make any man "praise God from whom all blessings flow."

name: Wooten, Annie S.
description: Two years ago G. N. & I. C. sent us this original structure of dynamic determination. That is to say, among the many things for which Annie is noted, determination easily takes the lead. And, it may truthfully be said Annie's gallantry of spirit is forever a source of wondrous admiration to her friends; likewise, a thing that any mortal would do well to emulate. She has a way of doing big things in an easy, graceful way as if she were accustomed to doing them every day in the year. And work! Her capacity for work is as unlimited as Professor Sanford's line of jokes at a mass meeting. We might say in passing that Annie is verily in love. Yes, in love! Now, her feeble biographer is into a "mess", so we will hastily add that the object of Miss Wooten's affections is no other than Professor —er—er—well, Professor Rosalie Rathbone. Fortunate indeed is Miss Rathbone! We don't know what "Annie S." contemplates doing when she gets her "dip", but we'll wager whatever it is, is done right.
